title: USI-EH27-G&P - Government & Public Services -L35 - Specialist Design - Graphic Designer - Hyderabad
description: G&P GPS Specialist Design - Graphic Designer As a Senior Graphic Designer, you will play a key role in the development of pursuit, marketing and other related design assets and collaterals for print and digital media; working closely with the offshore and onshore stakeholders. Being a great team player, creative, flexible, and efficient is key to success.Work you'll doAs a Specialist, Design on the Government & Public Services Pursuit Center of Excellence team, you will be responsible for: Develop concepts and creative solutions for client-facing, marketing, and pursuit-related design projects Create customized deliverables that align with stakeholder expectations and Deloitte brand identity guidelines Design storyboards, visual themes, illustrations, layouts, brochures, infographics, presentations, and other promotional materials Partner with offshore and onshore stakeholders through project scoping, execution, and delivery Manage multiple design assignments across timelines while maintaining quality and brand consistency The teamThe Government & Public Services Pursuit Center of Excellence supports strategic pursuit and marketing efforts through high-impact design, storytelling, and visual communication. The team works closely with offshore and onshore stakeholders to develop branded materials that help teams communicate clearly and competitively. This group partners across functions to deliver creative assets for proposals, presentations, and other business development initiatives.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 2:00 PM to 11:00 PMQualificationsRequired: Graduate or post-graduate degree in Fine Arts, Visual Communication, Media, Communication, Graphic Design, or a related field 3-6 years of experience in creative design or visual communication design within marketing or marketing communications Portfolio demonstrating design work across print and digital media Experience using Adobe InDesign, Adobe Illustrator, Adobe Photoshop, and Microsoft Office Suite Experience creating brochures, infographics, presentations, storyboards, and other promotional materials Experience working with corporate brand identity standards and brand guidelines Experience translating complex information or analytics into visual communications Preferred: Degree in Graphic Design or Visual Communication Experience with Adobe Digital Publishing Suite (DPS) or iBook Author Experience with responsive web design tools and technologies, including HyperText Markup Language 5 (HTML5), jQuery, Cascading Style Sheets (CSS), Adobe Dreamweaver, Adobe Muse, or Adobe Edge Experience with motion graphics, video editing, or audio editing using Adobe After Effects, Adobe Premiere, or Adobe AuditionHow you’ll grow At Deloitte, we’ve invested a great deal to create a rich environment in which our professionals can grow. We want all our people to develop in their own way, playing to their own strengths as they hone their leadership skills. And, as a part of our efforts, we provide our professionals with a variety of learning and networking opportunities— including exposure to leaders, sponsors, coaches, and challenging assignments—to help accelerate their careers along the way. No two people learn in exactly the same way. So, we provide a range of resources including live classrooms, team-based learning, and eLearning. DU: The Leadership Center in India, our state-of-the-art, worldclass learning Center in the Hyderabad offices is an extension of the Deloitte University (DU) in Westlake, Texas, and represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to our people’s growth and development. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India  Benefits At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. We value our people and offer employees a broad range of benefits. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you.   Deloitte’s culture Our positive and supportive culture encourages our people to do their best work every day. We celebrate individuals by recognizing their uniqueness and offering them the flexibility to make daily choices that can help them to be healthy, centered, confident, and aware. We offer well-being programs and are continuously looking for new ways to maintain a culture that is inclusive, invites authenticity, leverages our diversity, and where our people excel and lead healthy, happy lives.  Learn more about Life at Deloitte.  Corporate citizenship Deloitte is led by a purpose: to make an impact that matters. This purpose defines who we are and extends to relationships with our clients, our people and our communities.  We believe that business has the power to inspire and transform. We focus on education, giving, skill-based volunteerism, and leadership to help drive positive social impact in our communities.  Learn more about Deloitte’s impact on the world.

title: USI Audit | EH | FY 27 | QRM | L45 - Deputy Manager, Risk and Regulatory Services
description: Deputy Manager, Risk and Regulatory ServicesWe are seeking an Assistant Manager to support the Assurance Nonattest Quality & Risk Management team within the Audit & Assurance business. This role supports quality monitoring, and risk-sensing activities across Assurance nonattest engagements and helps teams adhere to internal policies, quality standards, and established processes.The professionals in this role will review monitoring results, identify trends and risk indicators, oversee reporting and issue tracking, and provide actionable insights to leadership and key stakeholders. The role also includes responsibility for reviewing team output, coaching junior professionals, strengthening execution quality, and contributing to continuous improvement of monitoring processes, and reporting capabilities.The ideal candidate will be a critical thinker with relevant experience in compliance, quality monitoring, reporting, analytics, engagement operations, or related business support environments.Work you'll do:As a Deputy Manager, Risk and Regulatory Services in the Assurance Nonattest Quality & Risk Management team, you will be responsible for supporting quality monitoring, reporting, and issue management activities across Assurance nonattest engagements.Key Responsibilities• Support execution of quality monitoring, and compliance tracking across Assurance nonattest engagements• Review monitoring results, exception outputs, and engagement-level information to identify policy/process non-adherence, quality risks, and negative trends• Perform and oversee data validation, data cleansing, and trend analysis to improve the reliability and usability of reporting outputs• Translate business, risk, process, and policy requirements into meaningful metrics, reporting views, and actionable insights• Track findings, remediation actions, aging items, and closure status to support timely follow-up and effective issue management• Prepare concise summaries, presentations, dashboards, and reports for leadership and key stakeholders• Review work performed by analysts and senior analysts to ensure accuracy, consistency, and adherence to team standards• Coach and guide junior team members on quality of work, analytical approach, judgment, and documentation standards• Help allocate work, monitor progress against deadlines, and support prioritization across multiple assignments• Collaborate with Quality & Risk Management (QRM) and engagement teams to strengthen processes and improve reporting effectiveness• Identify opportunities for process improvement and efficiency through analysis of operational and performance data• Contribute to continuous improvement of methodologies, templates, governance routines, and analytics capabilitiesThe teamThe Assurance Nonattest Quality & Risk Management team supports the Audit & Assurance business by monitoring quality, identifying risk indicators, and helping teams operate within established policies and processes. The team works closely with Quality & Risk Management and engagement stakeholders to provide reporting, issue tracking, and insights that strengthen execution quality and support continuous improvement across nonattest engagements.Location : HyderabadQualifications & Expectations• Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, Economics, Data Analytics, or a related field• 6+ years of experience in engagement operations, project management, quality monitoring, compliance, reporting, or data analytics• Experience reviewing work, coaching junior team members, and supporting team delivery quality• Experience creating leadership-level reporting, analyses, and presentations using Microsoft Excel and Microsoft PowerPoint• Experience with dashboard development, data modeling/analytics, and report creation. Familiarity with Tableau/Power BI is preferred• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to interpret data and translate findings into practical recommendations• Experience in Assurance-related services, compliance, risk, quality tracking, reporting, data analytics, or related business operations• Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to navigate within and across cross-functional teams• High attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities in a deadline-driven environment with minimalsupervision • Awareness of product development concepts, including ideation, testing, and release cycles. Familiarity with Agile methodology is preferred• You should expect to co-locate with team members or other colleagues in a Deloitte office, and/or virtually, based on specific team and business expectationsSuccess in This Role Will Be Measured By• Timely completion of monitoring and reporting activities• Accuracy, quality, and usability of dashboards, reports, and outputs• Value of insights and trend analysis provided to stakeholders• Effective tracking, escalation, and closure of findings and remediation actions• Quality, consistency, and timeliness of team deliverables• Effectiveness in coaching junior team members and improving overall team execution quality• Ability to work independently, demonstrate ownership, and uphold high ethical standards

title: USI FY27 | Cyber Operate- Firewall Security Engineer- Consultant
description: Deloitte Cyber understands the unique challenges and opportunities businesses face in cybersecurity. Join our team to deliver powerful insights to help our clients navigate the ever-changing threat design and technology as we partner with clients to transform finance. Work you'll do As a Consultant on the Cyber Operate team, you will be responsible for: Providing architecture analysis, design, implementation, and operational support for enterprise firewall environments, with a primary focus on Palo Alto firewalls Troubleshooting complex firewall, routing, Network Address Translation (NAT), Virtual Private Network (VPN), and network connectivity issues across enterprise environments Administering firewall policy changes, audits, rule analysis, and governance activities to support security baselines and least-privilege access Supporting migrations, upgrades, high availability, and production and non-production implementations across firewall and network security environments Creating technical documentation, standard operating procedures, and runbooks while supporting client meetings, architecture reviews, and security assessments Providing level 2 and level 3 on-call support and collaborating with network, cloud, infrastructure, and security teams to maintain secure and resilient network controls The team Deloitte's Cyber Operate teams manage clients' critical cyber assets either as a fully managed service or in partnership with clients. They deliver skilled talent, cutting-edge technologies, and robust processes to operate client cyber capabilities. This includes: Managing Identity Lifecycle Security Operations Threat Intelligence Application Security Business Transformation Continuous Compliance. Cyber Operate team focuses on efficiently running operations by enabling automation, reducing manual effort and adhering to Service Level Agreements (SLAs) based on the standards set by clients Location: Bengaluru / Hyderabad / Pune / Chennai Shift Timings: 24x7 rotational shifts, including night, weekend, holiday, and on-call coverage based on project assignment Qualifications Required: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Cyber Security, Information Security, Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field 3-6 years of experience in firewall implementation, operations, engineering, and troubleshooting Experience with Palo Alto firewall administration, including Panorama, rule analysis, rule modification, and policy optimization Experience with firewall platforms such as Palo Alto Networks, Check Point, Cisco ASA or Firepower, and Fortinet Experience with Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol (TCP/IP) networking, including routing, switching, NAT, VPNs, and VLANs Experience with packet analysis, traffic flow review, and network trace analysis using packet capture tools Experience with Information Technology Infrastructure Library (ITIL) operational processes, including change management, incident management, problem management, and configuration management Preferred: Firewall certifications such as Palo Alto ACE, PCNSE, CCNP Security, CCSA or CCSE, Fortinet NSE, Cisco CyberOps Associate, or CCNA Security Experience with cloud firewall and hybrid network security architecture Experience supporting Cisco ASA to Palo Alto migration activities Experience with firewall rule recertification tools such as AlgoSec or FireMon Experience using Python, Representational State Transfer (REST) application programming interfaces, or similar tools for deployment, reporting, or configuration tasks Experience leading client engagements or serving in a lead technical role#Cyber_Cyber Operate

title: USI- EH27- Operations- TBM FINANCE - Hyderabad_ Financial Planning and Analysis
description: Specialist Manager, Financial Planning and AnalysisAs Deloitte Technology’s Technology Finance function continues to scale, this Manager role exists to lead a Center of Excellence (COE): building a high-performing team, driving financial planning and reporting quality, and serving as the accountable leader who connects team execution with global stakeholder priorities.You will partner with Service Area leaders and technology leaders, manage and grow a team of financial analysts, and ensure that deliverables meet the quality standards expected at Level 5 ITFM maturity. You will also lead your team's journey into AI-assisted workflows — building the capability, confidence, and culture that turns technology curiosity into real efficiency gains. If you are a strong financial professional who leads people well and thrives in a global, fast-paced environment — this is a rare opportunity to build something.What You'll DoFinancial Planning & Analysis• Lead financial planning, forecasting, reporting, and analysis activities for assigned stakeholders and business areas, ensuring accuracy and executive-level clarity in all deliverables.• Perform and review complex financial analysis, variance reporting, and reconciliations to identify trends, issues, and efficiency opportunities.• Develop financial narratives that help technology leaders understand cost drivers, including non-controllable factors.• Prepare clear, polished updates and communications for US and regional leadership on deliverables, risks, team capacity, and operational performance.Stakeholder Management• Partner with Service Area/Service line/Cost center leaders to provide financial analysis, planning support, and ad hoc insights that enable confident and fact-based decisions.• Coordinate with cross-functional team members, business stakeholders, and finance partners to address financial questions accurately and promptly.• Support the preparation of executive-ready financial presentations, translating analytical work into clear narratives for senior technology and business audiences.Cost Category Management & Governance• Own end-to-end financial management for an assigned cost category (e.g., labor, cloud, hardware, or software), ensuring accurate tracking, reporting, and accountability across the full budget lifecycle.• Lead detailed variance analysis, trend monitoring, and cost driver reporting for the assigned category, translating data into actionable insights for technology and finance leadership.• Govern master data integrity for the assigned cost category, including WBS codes, cost hierarchies, and allocation rules, ensuring alignment to ITFM/TBM standards and reporting accuracy.Cross-Functional Intelligence• Collaborate across Technology service areas to gather financial intelligence on activities, contracts, and purchases that could impact cost category budgets — enabling proactive planning and stakeholder readiness.• Identify and escalate financial risks or data quality issues early, maintaining the reporting integrity expected at Level 5 ITFM maturity.Local Team Leadership & Development• Oversee work assignment, delegation, supervision, and quality review for India based analysts supporting the COE Financials function.• Manage existing team members by setting priorities, providing coaching, monitoring performance, and supporting career development.• Lead hiring, onboarding, and integration of new team members to build a strong, scalable local delivery capability.• Establish consistent processes, operating rhythms, and quality standards across the team — creating a delivery model that stakeholders can depend on.• Identify process improvements that increase consistency, efficiency, and transparency in financial and operational reporting.AI & Innovation AdoptionOur team is expected to be at the leading edge of integrating AI and automation into its daily work. As Manager, you own that journey for your team.• Champion AI tool exploration and adoption — identifying where generative AI, automation platforms, and analytical assistants can reduce manual effort in reporting, reconciliation, and data governance workflows.• Create an environment where experimentation is encouraged and failure is a learning event — build the psychological safety and operational space for analysts to try new tools, share what they discover, and continuously improve how work gets done.• Identify automation opportunities within local delivery workflows — look critically at repetitive, high-volume tasks (data pulls, format standardization, variance tagging) and develop a plan to eliminate or reduce them through automation.• Develop your team's AI literacy alongside their financial acumen — ensure analysts are building skills in AI-assisted tools as part of their professional growth, not as a side project. Make it part of onboarding, coaching, and performance conversations.• Coordinate with senior leadership and peer managers on AI tooling strategy — share learnings, align on tool choices, and ensure the team's adoption contributes to a coherent, firm-appropriate approach across the global team.• Stay current on AI capabilities relevant to finance and operations — bring informed perspective to conversations about what is emerging, what is ready, and what requires caution.Who You'll Work WithThis role bridges local leadership and global stakeholder service:• Technology Service Area/Cost center Leaders• India-based Financial Analysts (direct reports)• Other TBM teams and COE’s• Finance & Accounting Partners• Director, Technology Finance• TBM CL5 Managers (peers)• Business Relationship Managers• Business LeadersRequired• 8+ years of experience in Technology Finance, FP&A, or technology business management• Experience leading or managing a team of financial professionals• Strong financial analysis skills including variance analysis, reconciliations, and cost allocation• Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English; ability to work effectively with globally distributed stakeholders• Demonstrated ability to build processes, establish operating standards, and drive consistency across a teamPreferred• ITFM/TBM platform experience (Apptio, ServiceNow ITFM, or equivalent); ATUM certification a plus• Experience with SAP, Qlikview/Qliksense, Tableau, or similar enterprise financial platforms• Familiarity with WBS structures, product hierarchies, and TCO modeling• Experience in a global or cross-border delivery model, supporting geographically distributed stakeholders and leadership teams• Hands-on experience with AI-assisted finance or operations tools — including automation platforms, generative AI writing tools, or data pipeline tools applied to financial workflows• A genuine track record of curiosity-driven adoption — examples of identifying a tool, piloting it, and scaling it within a team environment• Ability to coach and develop others in new technology — experience building team capability in tools or processes, not just using them personally

title: USI FY27 | Cyber Operate- Firewall Security Engineer - Senior Consultant
description: Deloitte Cyber understands the unique challenges and opportunities businesses face in cybersecurity. Join our team to deliver powerful insights to help our clients navigate the ever-changing threat design and technology as we partner with clients to transform finance.Work you'll doAs a Senior Consultant on the Cyber Operate team, you will be responsible for: Providing architecture analysis, design, implementation, and operational support for enterprise firewall environments, with a primary focus on Palo Alto firewalls Conducting firewall audits, rule analysis, risk reviews, and policy governance activities to support security baselines and least-privilege access Troubleshooting firewall, routing, Network Address Translation (NAT), Virtual Private Network (VPN), and network connectivity issues across enterprise environments Supporting migrations, upgrades, high availability, and configuration changes across Palo Alto and Cisco ASA firewall environments Creating standard operating procedures, technical documentation, implementation guides, and operational runbooks Providing level 2 and level 3 support, including on-call support, and collaborating with network, infrastructure, cloud, and security teams during delivery and client engagements The teamDeloitte's Cyber Operate teams manage clients' critical cyber assets either as a fully managed service or in partnership with clients. They deliver skilled talent, cutting-edge technologies, and robust processes to operate client cyber capabilities. This includes: Managing Identity Lifecycle Security Operations Threat Intelligence Application Security Business Transformation Continuous Compliance. Cyber Operate team focuses on efficiently running operations by enabling automation, reducing manual effort and adhering to Service Level Agreements (SLAs) based on the standards set by clientsLocation: Bengaluru / Hyderabad / Pune / ChennaiShift Timings: 24x7 rotational shifts, including night, weekend, and holiday coverage; on-call support required based on project assignmentQualificationsRequired: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Cyber Security, Information Security, Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field 6-10 years of experience in firewall implementation, operations, engineering, and troubleshooting Experience with Palo Alto firewall administration, including Panorama, rule analysis, rule modification, and policy optimization Experience with firewall platforms such as Palo Alto Networks, Check Point, Cisco ASA or Firepower, and Fortinet Experience with Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol (TCP/IP) networking, including routing, switching, NAT, VPNs, and VLANs Experience with packet analysis, traffic flow identification, and network trace analysis using packet capture tools Experience with Information Technology Infrastructure Library (ITIL) operational processes, including change management, incident management, problem management, and configuration management Preferred: Firewall certifications such as Palo Alto ACE, Palo Alto Networks Certified Network Security Engineer (PCNSE), CCNP Security, CCSA or CCSE, Fortinet NSE, Cisco CyberOps Associate, or CCNA Security Experience leading client engagements or serving in a lead technical role Experience with cloud firewall and hybrid network security architecture Experience with Python, Representational State Transfer (REST) application programming interfaces, or similar tools for deployment, reporting, or configuration tasks Experience with firewall rule recertification tools such as AlgoSec or FireMon Experience developing software or version compliance reports#Cyber_Cyber Operate

title: USI- EH27- Operations- TBM FINANCE - Hyderabad_ Specialist, Financial Planning and Analysis
description: Specialist, Financial Planning and Analysis – Deloitte Support Services India Private LimitedLocation: Hyderabad Experience: 3–5 yearsWork you’ll doAs a CL 3 professional at Senior Analyst level in IT Business Analysis, this role will support Technology Business Management (TBM) and FP&A activities for the Information Technology Services (ITS) budget and Other Cost, Telecom, TEM (Travel, Entertainment & Meetings) and Reporting COE Center of Excellence (OTT CoE). The position will focus on budgeting, forecasting, financial reporting, variance analysis, cost tracking, and preparation of management reporting packs. The ideal candidate should be detail-oriented, analytical, and comfortable working with large datasets while collaborating with internal stakeholders to support budget planning and execution effectivelyResponsibilities•Support budgeting, forecasting, and financial reporting for ITS and OTT CoE•Conduct variance analysis and provide actionable financial insights•Build and maintain Excel-based financial models, trackers, and reports•Track operational and capital expenditures and ensure data accuracy•Collaborate with internal stakeholders to align budget planning and execution•Assist in preparing monthly and quarterly financial review packs•Support finance operations through data analysis and reporting activities•Ensure accuracy, consistency, and timeliness of financial informationRequired Skills / Experience•3–5 years of experience in Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A)•Strong understanding of financial statements and cost center budgeting•Strong Excel and reporting skills – pivot tables, formulas, VBA etc.•Ability to work with large and complex datasets•Strong communication and stakeholder management skills•Strong analytical and problem-solving capability•Ability to work effectively in a dynamic and deadline-driven environment independentlyPreferred Qualifications•Prior experience supporting ITS budgets•Exposure to Technology-related financial processes•Knowledge of Reporting, Automation using VBA, Python programming•Experience in SAP ERP and SAP (S4) HANA and AO reports as end user•Experience with QlikView, Tableau, Power BI is a plus but not required•Familiarity with PowerPoint for reporting and presentations•Familiarity with AI tools (Chat GPT, Claude) to prompt and build efficiency in processEducation•Bachelor’s degree in finance, Accounting, Commerce, Business Administration, Information Systems, or a related field•Relevant finance or analytics certification preferredExperience•3–5 years of relevant experience in FP&A, financial reporting, budgeting, forecasting, or business/financial analysis•Experience working with finance data, reporting tools, and cross-functional stakeholders•Experience in technology finance, IT budgeting, or internal business support environments will be an advantageInterview Process•Excel Skills Test – assessment to evaluate practical Excel knowledge•Personal Interview – discussion on FP&A experience, domain knowledge, and communication skillsShift Timing2.00PM to 11.00 PMThe teamAt Deloitte, we are all about collaboration. And nowhere is this more apparent than among our internal services teams. With combined specialist skills, these teams provide the essential support and advice our client-facing colleagues need across the firm. This enables them to focus their efforts on delivering the best possible service to clients. Across Human Resources, Clients & Industries, Finance & Legal, Practice Support Services, Quality & Risk Services, IT Services, and Workplace Services & Real Estate, the teams collectively deliver the Deloitte experienceHow you will growAt Deloitte, we invest in creating an environment where professionals can grow in their own way, build on their strengths, and continue developing leadership capabilities. Our professionals benefit from a variety of learning and networking opportunities, including exposure to leaders, sponsors, coaches, and challenging assignments. We provide multiple learning formats, including live classrooms, team-based learning, and eLearning, to support continuous developmentBenefitsAt Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. We value our people and offer a broad range of benefits designed to support their professional and personal well-beingDeloitte’s cultureOur positive and supportive culture encourages our people to do their best work every day. We recognize individuals for their unique strengths and provide flexibility and well-being support to help our people remain healthy, centered, confident, and aware. We continue to foster a culture that is inclusive, invites authenticity, leverages diversity, and enables our people to excelCorporate citizenshipDeloitte is led by a purpose: to make an impact that matters. This purpose extends to our relationships with clients, our people, and our communities. We believe business has the power to inspire and transform, and we focus on education, giving, skill-based volunteerism, and leadership to help drive positive social impact in our communitiesAbout DeloitteDeloitte refers to one or more of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited, a UK private company limited by guarantee, its network of member firms, and their related entities. title: USI - EH27 - F&A - Business services - PPMD Admin - Associate Analyst- Hyderabad/Mumbai/Bengaluru
description: The Administrative Assistant provides comprehensive administrative support to Partners, Principals, Managing Directors (PPMD), and Senior Managers. This role demands exceptional communication, interpersonal, and execution skills. The Administrative Assistant is responsible for promoting effective teamwork and fostering a collaborative work environment, while efficiently utilizing resources without sacrificing quality. Proficiency in Microsoft Office is required. The ideal candidate is highly organized, task-oriented, and adept at prioritizing multiple assignments and stakeholders. Strong interpersonal skills are essential, with the ability to establish credibility and collaborate across all levels of the firm. Attention to detail, excellent follow-through, superior communication skills, and a positive, professional demeanor are critical for success in this position.Work you'll doAs a Services Support Associate II, Corporate Services on the USI ESC Admin Support Team, you will be responsible for:Performing administrative tasks involving confidential and sensitive informationManaging leader calendars, including scheduling and rescheduling meetingsPreparing, editing, and distributing memos, letters, emails, and other correspondenceCoordinating domestic and international travel arrangements, including itineraries, lodging, local transportation, and visa or passport supportPreparing and reconciling time and expense reports and supporting audits as neededCoordinating leadership events, client visits, and other administrative activities with national and local teamsThe teamThe USI ESC Admin Support Team offers comprehensive administrative assistance to PPMDs and Senior Leaders within the USI firm. Their responsibilities include managing calendars, coordinating travel arrangements, facilitating time and expense submissions, and supporting client and leadership visits on a daily basis, in addition to managing any other general administrative tasks as required for the role.Location: Hyderabad, Bengaluru, Gurgaon, MumbaiQualificationsRequired:Bachelor’s degree1 year of administrative experience in a corporate environmentExperience managing calendars, meetings, and correspondence for senior stakeholdersExperience preparing time and expense reportsExperience coordinating domestic and international travel arrangementsProficiency in Microsoft OfficeAbility to work overtime on weeknights and weekends, as neededPreferred:Experience supporting Partners, Principals, Managing Directors, or Senior ManagersExperience coordinating leadership events or client visitsExperience working with confidential business information

title: USI-EH27-TEE-Specialist Manager, Analytics and Insights - Hyderabad
description: Your role will focus on translating talent data into actionable insights that support strategic decisions, workforce planning, and talent operations. As a Specialist Manager, Analytics and Insights, you will partner with Talent leaders and cross-functional stakeholders to design data-driven solutions, shape reporting strategy, and deliver executive-ready insights. This role is ideal for a professional who combines analytics, stakeholder management, and storytelling to help drive informed decision-making across the talent lifecycle. Work you'll do As a Specialist Manager, Analytics and Insights on the People Analytics team, you will be responsible for: Partnering with stakeholders across the talent lifecycle to design data solutions that support business decisions and talent operations Developing executive-ready analytics, dashboards, visualizations, and presentations using tools such as PowerPoint, Tableau, and One Model Leading strategic reporting and analytics initiatives, including roadmap development, project coordination, and cross-team delivery Supporting complex analyses and high-priority data requests from senior leaders to enable informed decision-making Managing junior team members while advising Talent leaders on opportunities identified through workforce data and insights The team The People Analytics team collaborates across Talent and the business to uncover data-driven insights to solve business challenges across the talent lifecycle and enable transformational change inside of Deloitte. Our teams (e.g., data science, surveys, candidate assessments, evaluation, sensing) provide a holistic and client-centric approach that complements our wide range of analytical tools and methods to identify valuable workforce insights which, in turn, fuels the business. This role engages directly with Talent leadership/teams to deliver on requests across products and services People Analytics offers. Client Solutions professionals are expected to act as data advisors, developing strong, concise points of view for leadership to support informed decision-making. We’re looking for a versatile professional to be a key player in our journey to deliver holistic data driven insights to our Talent leaders and teams. Location: Hyderabad Shift Timings: 2 PM to 11 PM Qualifications Required: Bachelor’s degree 10+ years of experience in project management, reporting, analytics, and data visualization Experience with data transformation or data modeling tools such as Tableau Prep or Structured Query Language (SQL) Experience creating executive-ready presentations, dashboards, and analytics deliverables Experience using Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, Word, and Teams in a business environment Experience aggregating and analyzing operational data to identify insights and trends Experience managing team members and coordinating deliverables across multiple stakeholders Preferred: Graduate degree

title: USI-EH27-DTTL-CoRe Procurement - Travel Manager - Hyderabad
description: Core Procurement - Procurement Travel Manager - HyderabadThe Specialist Manager, Procurement and Category Management will support Deloitte USI travel operations and procurement activities, with a focus on category management, supplier coordination, travel program execution, and stakeholder support. This role requires collaboration across geographies and functions to help manage travel services, monitor supplier performance, identify improvement opportunities, and support business priorities through data-driven insights. The role also involves oversight of travel tools, operational processes, and travel safety and security programs.Work you'll doAs a Specialist Manager, Procurement and Category Management on the Travel Operations and Procurement team, you will be responsible for: Supporting Deloitte USI travel operations and procurement activities across air, hotel, ground transportation, and other travel categories Coordinating implementation and administration of travel programs, online booking tools, and travel-related technologies Managing travel data from multiple sources to identify trends, gaps, and improvement opportunities and provide timely reporting to leadership and stakeholders Monitoring supplier and Travel Management Company performance against defined metrics and supporting supplier relationship management activities Communicating travel program requirements, security protocols, and operational updates to stakeholders and travelers across the organization Managing team members and supporting cross-functional initiatives to improve process efficiency, traveler experience, and internal client satisfaction The teamThe Travel Operations and Procurement team supports Deloitte USI’s business travel program through supplier management, program operations, policy support, and process improvement. The team works closely with internal stakeholders, global travel teams, and external suppliers to help deliver efficient, compliant, and scalable travel services. This role contributes to procurement strategy, operational execution, and continuous improvement across the travel category.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM / 11:00 AM to 8:00 PMQualificationsRequired: 10+ years of experience in travel procurement, travel operations, category management, or supplier management Experience managing air, hotel, ground transportation, and travel program suppliers Experience working with online booking tools, travel management processes, and travel-related technologies Experience analyzing data and preparing reports using Microsoft Excel and Microsoft PowerPoint Experience managing stakeholder communications across multiple business levels and geographies Experience managing direct reports or leading workstreams English written and verbal communication skills Preferred: Experience in travel procurement within large corporate environments Experience supporting stakeholders in the Asia Pacific region Experience documenting and improving business processes Experience managing issue escalation and resolution Experience conducting market research across travel categories Experience working across cross-functional teams in a multi-priority environment #ESG-CORE27

title: USI-EH27-MFUK HRS-Learning Admin-Services Support Associate I-Talent Processes and Business Support
description: HRS Learning & Talent Support Services Associate Analyst – Deloitte Support Services India Private LimitedDo you enjoy learning administration? Then this may be the perfect opportunity for you!In the HRS Learning & Talent Support Services Associate Analyst role, you will provide support to the team in the delivery of an excellent level of service to the North-South Europe (NSE) firm. The role requires you to provide high quality first line learning administration support to the HRS Learning & Talent Support Services team, the emphasis is to provide a consistently high level of customer service to deliver solutions that meet the needs of our users and HR Services objectives.Work you’ll do.As a part of this team, you will be responsible for:? Adding classes to the Deloitte Learning Management System? Processing of all scheduled reports? Provide delegate list.? Distribution of electronic training materials and pre-work;? Closing classes and processing of post course evaluations and feedback;? Ensure all system related activities or updates are completed accurately in time deadlines and in line with procedural guidelines;? Ensure compliance with appropriate Data Protection legislation at all times? Working within Service Level and Operational Level Agreements;? Administration of other Learning Support Services processes as requiredKey skills required.? The successful candidate will be able to demonstrate a professional, friendly and approachable manner? They should also be able to work using their own initiative.? Enthusiastic and eager to learn new technologies.? Committed to working with their team to maintain a positive team spirit.? Good listener with a calm manner, and the ability to ask effective questions.? Able to communicate in writing at all levels, displaying good email etiquette.? Required to always project a professional image of the Practice Support Services, and shows an interest in the external industry.? Must be able to demonstrate a logical approach to problems.? Can act with urgency when necessary.? Is open, receptive, and adaptable to change.? Demonstrates integrity and a can-do attitude.Work Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 2 PM to 11 PMThe USI Supporting UK CBS HR Services team at Hyderabad supports the Deloitte organization in the UK with a wide variety of capabilities like financial reporting, HR and financial transaction processing, administrative service and helpdesk support, marketing and IT services.QualificationsRequired:• Graduate degree• 0-1 years of work experience in learning administration, back office service operations, customer service operations• Proficient in the use of Microsoft Office package applicationsPreferred:• Prior experience in training and development domainHow you’ll growAt Deloitte, we’ve invested a great deal to create a rich environment in which our professionals can grow. We want all our people to develop in their own way, playing to their own strengths as they hone their leadership skills. And, as a part of our efforts, we provide our professionals with a variety of learning and networking opportunities—including exposure to leaders, sponsors, coaches, and challenging assignments—to help accelerate their careers along the way. No two people learn in exactly the same way. So, we provide a range of resources including live classrooms, team-based learning, and eLearning. DU: The Leadership Center in India, our state-of-the-art, world-class learning Center in the Hyderabad offices is an extension of the Deloitte University (DU) in Westlake, Texas, and represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to our people’s growth and development. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in IndiaBenefitsAt Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. We value our people and offer employees a broad range of benefits. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you.Deloitte’s cultureOur positive and supportive culture encourages our people to do their best work every day. We celebrate individuals by recognizing their uniqueness and offering them the flexibility to make daily choices that can help them to be healthy, centered, confident, and aware. We offer well-being programs and are continuously looking for new ways to maintain a culture that is inclusive, invites authenticity, leverages our diversity, and where our people excel and lead healthy, happy lives. Learn more about Life at Deloitte.Corporate citizenshipDeloitte is led by a purpose: to make an impact that matters. This purpose defines who we are and extends to relationships with our clients, our people and our communities. We believe that business has the power to inspire and transform. We focus on education, giving, skill-based volunteerism, and leadership to help drive positive social impact in our communities. Learn more about Deloitte’s impact on the world.#mfnsehrsref

Then this may be the perfect opportunity for you!In the HRS Learning & Talent Support Services Associate Analyst role, you will provide support to the team in the delivery of an excellent level of service to the North-South Europe (NSE) firm. Ensure all system related activities or updates are completed accurately in time deadlines and in line with procedural guidelines;? Ensure compliance with appropriate Data Protection legislation at all times? Working within Service Level and Operational Level Agreements;? Administration of other Learning Support Services processes as requiredKey skills required.? The successful candidate will be able to demonstrate a professional, friendly and approachable manner? They should also be able to work using their own initiative.? Enthusiastic and eager to learn new technologies.? Committed to working with their team to maintain a positive team spirit.? Good listener with a calm manner, and the ability to ask effective questions.? Able to communicate in writing at all levels, displaying good email etiquette.? Required to always project a professional image of the Practice Support Services, and shows an interest in the external industry.? Must be able to demonstrate a logical approach to problems.? Can act with urgency when necessary.? Is open, receptive, and adaptable to change.? Demonstrates integrity and a can-do attitude.Work Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 2 PM to 11 PMThe USI Supporting UK CBS HR Services team at Hyderabad supports the Deloitte organization in the UK with a wide variety of capabilities like financial reporting, HR and financial transaction processing, administrative service and helpdesk support, marketing and IT services.QualificationsRequired:• Graduate degree• 0-1 years of work experience in learning administration, back office service operations, customer service operations• Proficient in the use of Microsoft Office package applicationsPreferred:• Prior experience in training and development domainHow you’ll growAt Deloitte, we’ve invested a great deal to create a rich environment in which our professionals can grow. title: USI-EH27- G&P-R&I-L40-Specialist Assistant Manager, Strategic Market Insights and Thought Leadership (TMT-TMT- AI Data Centers, Semiconductors, and Tech Hardware Research)-Hyderabad
description: Specialist Assistant Manager, Strategic Market Insights and Thought Leadership (TMT- AI Data Centers, Semiconductors, and Tech Hardware Research)  Deloitte’s Center for TMT is one of the industry teams within the Research & Insights (R&I) group, focusing primarily on the TMT industry. The Center's goals are to:Assess opportunities and challenges facing companies in the TMT industryProvide practical and actionable insights - whether they are strategic, technical, operational.Bring together a range of stakeholders to address issues impacting the health and evolution of the TMT industry.Make extensive use of leading practices from TMT professionals across diverse disciplines, including content strategy, digital distribution, advertising and monetization, consumer analytics, rights management, finance, risk management, regulation, and human capital, to identify innovative steps they have taken to address critical challenges.Learn more about the TMT Center and have a peek into our work here.This role involves contributing to thought leadership and research projects across the full lifecycle—from design to delivery—examining critical challenges and opportunities in the Technology sector with particular focus primarily on semiconductor and enterprise hardware sub-sectors (including AI data center infrastructure), as well as consumer tech (secondary focus). The candidate will contribute to advancing the quality of the Center's research and thought-leadership work in Technology sector, with a focus on AI tech infrastructure (e.g., data centers, memory, GPUs, networking, edge, etc.), hybrid computing architectures, advanced packaging of hardware and chips, AI in chip design and manufacturing, strategic supply chain chokepoints, and adoption of AI/agent powered consumer devices and edge applications. These include whitepapers, in-depth research reports, case studies, articles, data-based research tools, client presentations, and collaterals for specific client accounts. These deliverables would integrate information from a range of primary and secondary sources, including Deloitte subject-matter experts, primary research (surveys), publicly available data, third-party research, analyst reports, presentations, industry commentaries, and news articles.  Responsibilities:As a Specialist Assistant Manager, focused on developing thought leadership, you will be required to –Work across diverse Tech sector (with a focus on semiconductor and enterprise hardware) topics, quickly build subject-matter familiarity, synthesize complex information, and deliver high-quality research insights.Develop and maintain subsector-level expertise across tech (and specifically hardware, semiconductor and consumer tech) landscape, including emerging technologies, business models, and financial drivers. This includes proactively researching companies, trends, themes, underlying technologies, and potential disruptions through direct study of industry reports, company disclosures and other credible sources.Identify novel topics, areas and trends to focus on, based on extensive analysis of competition thoughtware/articles and importance of issues based on market studies, industry reports and tracking.Design research proposals, build consensus, and formulate a research plan. Execute structured research plans by translating business objectives into clear research questions, identifying areas of investigation, and conducting thorough secondary research using relevant and trustworthy industry, company, and news sources.Interact with internal and external subject matter experts on industry topics and synthesize takeaways and findings into concise and well-structured narratives, assisting senior research leads to shape internal/external thought-leadership content and collaterals – through a combination of expertise and direct study, complemented by AI tools.Liaison with U.S. colleagues and senior researchers to understand, refine, and manage internal client expectations and priorities. Apply established research tools and methodologies to support business decision-making. Work on varied data sets (e.g., survey data, publicly available data sets) to derive data-driven insights.Identify and use the relevant data analysis methods and work with the relevant data science and analytics professionals to analyze voluminous and complex datasets. Demonstrate strong writing skills as required for thought leadership reports. Display strong consultative and engaging presentation skills.Being largely autonomous (with minimal supervision) and managing the projects to ensure outcomes and meet deadlines, demonstrating a high level of ownership and accountability.Assist senior researchers and practice leaders with internal briefings and external client meetings/webinars – by performing customized data analysis and presenting results and insights into client-ready briefing decks.Ability to apply sound research judgment by grounding outputs in direct study and critical thinking, while using generative AI tools to enhance efficiency (and not as a substitute for independent synthesis.)Develop an understanding of Deloitte’s solutions, services, and offerings Requisite experience and skillsExperience6-9 years of experience in supporting/managing research projects, preferably in Technology industry related topics and areas as noted earlier in this document.Experience working with US/global clients and teams is preferred. Since the job requires working closely with US-based colleagues, the ability to work across cultures is important. Industry/sector/subsector skillsBusiness research/thought-leadership development in Technology sector is preferred. Knowledge of AI[1]driven chip demand, AI chips and accelerators that support data centers, role of AI in chip design and manufacturing, chip hardware and integrated systems’ time-to-market, and fab efficiency.Familiarity with architectural innovation, heterogeneous compute. (CPU/GPU/NPU/memory/interconnects), chiplet-based designs, co-packaged optics, advanced chip packaging and their role in performance and cost curves.Awareness about strategic chokepoints and importance of supply chain resilience for hardware and semiconductor components; Geopolitical and operational risks in this space.Consumer and end-user needs and attitudes towards AI and agent-powered devices and applications for work, education, and personal reasons – including AI at the edge. Technical skillsProficiency in using one or more of the research tools/databases, including S&P Global, Factiva, Thomson Research, Refinitiv Workspace, and Gartner. Sound analytical skills with experience in one or more of the following research methods: Primary research (surveys), secondary research, academic research, and case study research. Strong data analysis skills with the ability to work with quantitative and qualitative data – interpret, analyze, visualize, and provide data-driven insights. Ability to use statistical and advanced data analysis methods to analyze large and complex datasets, including surveys.Ability to develop compelling and analytical data visualizations using one or more tools like Excel, Tableau, Power BI, or others.Strong verbal and written communication skills. Problem-solving and critical thinking skills to generate meaningful analytical insights. Broad understanding of strategy models and frameworks, and the ability to apply them to research problems.Ability to understand the impact of marketplace changes on the industry, sector, or sub-sector. Proficiency in using generative AI-based platforms and tools in a responsible and transparent way to elevate research discovery, data insights, analytical rigor, and business writing.Proficiency in using generative AI and related tools to identify early signals and trends shaping the market through continuous sensing will be preferred.Familiarity with building custom GPTs and custom gen-AI research tools will be an added advantageThe Deloitte Research and Insights teams’ mission is to conduct research and analysis to develop compelling, forward-looking perspectives that enable clients to anticipate, prepare for, and respond to “what’s coming next.” The team plays an integral role in strengthening the firm’s strategic positioning by developing high-quality thoughtware from end to end, published on Deloitte Insights and Deloitte.com.Their work includes:Managing relations with internal stakeholders.Using advanced data science and survey advisory capabilities to strengthen the credibility of insights.Using editorial and visualization capabilities to improve the stickiness of firm thoughtware. Qualifications, experience, work location, and timingAcademic qualification: Master’s Degree like MBA, or Post Grad degree in Management / Economics / FinanceBachelor’s Degree in Computer Sciences / Tech / Software Engineering and related areas [preferred/recommended]Work experience: 6-9 yearsLocation: Hyderabad Work hours: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM IST (expected to stretch during busy periods)

title: USI-EH27-GTO-Learning Tools-Analyst
description: Tools AnalystAre you a detail-oriented, customer-focused individual who enjoys talking to clients to understand their learning requirement and analyze their areas of challenges to provide appropriate solution?  External tools team provides guidance to member firms on any issues related to the use of digital learning tools for devising learning solutions. Our team culture is collaborative and encourages team members to take initiative and seek on-the-job learning opportunities. Our professionals are committed to excellence and provide world class support service to global member firms at every juncture.Work you’ll doPrimarily responsible for providing support on global content development tools; user education, tools upgrades, promotional activities, and users with support and advisory requests. Works closely with GTO and learning network to maintain up-to-date information on global learning sites.•        Manage day to day queries on tools support by responding appropriately and escalating to vendors or other internal teams as needed.•        Manage development tools and perform administrative tasks around tools related webinars, user group meetings etc.•        Coordinate and participate in the testing of new tools or tool upgrades. •        Troubleshoot issues related to authoring tools (Brainshark, Articulate, FLIP, D-Learn).•        Ensure all content and tools related information is up to date on SharePoint COE sites, our global intranet pages.•        Support the design of tools training program •        Conduct research and share findings on latest trends in the field of learning designThe teamOur team culture is collaborative and encourages team members to take initiative and seek on-the-job learning opportunities. Our professionals are committed to excellence and to enhance the trust of the entire talent network. Quality is our top priority, and by focusing on exceptional client service, we continue to raise the bar on quality and deliver greater value to our clients.SkillsQualifications: •        Graduation in any stream, (2 – 3) years’ experience with knowledge centered support practices •        Working knowledge in authoring tools, such as Brainshark, Articulate Studio, Storyline, etc. Skills Required: •        Self-driven•        Good searching and analytical skills•        Advanced level of expertise with Microsoft PowerPoint. Working knowledge of Office applications and inter/intra-net search techniques needed•        Good presentation & visualization skills•        Experience in learning new or proprietary tools / technologies•        Experience troubleshooting project or technology issues until resolution•        Experience in delivering training, both classroom and via online channels•        Experience working with a global team in a virtual environment is preferred •        Client management skills and the ability to work with various clients across geographies (preferred)•        Excellent written and verbal communication - capable at communicating ideas and solutions through either a written format (e.g. email) or verbal formats (e.g., face-to-face or via telephone); ability to communicate need for clarification and guidance from management•        Experience working with Salesforce is preferred, but not required•        Prior experience in graphic designing and video editing software is an advantage•        Understanding of the Deloitte organization•        Familiarity with Saba Cloud learning management system, conceptual knowledge of HTML 5, JavaScript, CSS is preferred•        Experience in Instructional design is preferredHow you’ll growAt Deloitte, we’ve invested a great deal to create a rich environment in which our professionals can grow. We want all our people to develop in their own way, playing to their own strengths as they hone their leadership skills. And, as a part of our efforts, we provide our professionals with a variety of learning and networking opportunities—including exposure to leaders, sponsors, coaches, and challenging assignments—to help accelerate their careers along the way. No two people learn in exactly the same way. So, we provide a range of resources including live classrooms, team-based learning, and eLearning. DU: The Leadership Center in India, our state-of-the-art, world-class learning Center in the Hyderabad offices is an extension of the Deloitte University (DU) in Westlake, Texas, and represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to our people’s growth and development. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in IndiaBenefitsAt Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. We value our people and offer employees a broad range of benefits. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you. Deloitte’s cultureOur positive and supportive culture encourages our people to do their best work every day. We celebrate individuals by recognizing their uniqueness and offering them the flexibility to make daily choices that can help them to be healthy, centered, confident, and aware. We offer well-being programs and are continuously looking for new ways to maintain a culture that is inclusive, invites authenticity, leverages our diversity, and where our people excel and lead healthy, happy lives.  Learn more about Life at Deloitte.Corporate citizenshipDeloitte is led by a purpose: to make an impact that matters. This purpose defines who we are and extends to relationships with our clients, our people and our communities.  We believe that business has the power to inspire and transform. We focus on education, giving, skill-based volunteerism, and leadership to help drive positive social impact in our communities.  Learn more about Deloitte’s impact on the world.

title: USI - EH27 - Talent Experience - Services Specialist - Data Preparation and Reporting - Hyderabad
description: Support Deloitte’s Talent leaders by turning workforce data into actionable insights that inform decisions across the talent lifecycle. In this role, you will develop reporting, dashboards, and visualizations that help stakeholders understand trends, evaluate needs, and identify opportunities for action. The role is suited for a professional with experience in analytics, data preparation, and storytelling who can work across multiple stakeholders in a dynamic environment. Work you'll do As a Services Specialist, Data Preparation and Reporting on the Talent Experience & Engagement People Analytics team, you will be responsible for: Supporting Talent leadership through strategic reporting, dashboarding, and analysis that informs data-driven decision-making Creating data visualizations, dashboards, and presentation materials using tools such as Tableau and PowerPoint to communicate insights clearly Performing analyses and responding to data requests from senior leaders and Talent stakeholders Collaborating across the talent lifecycle to design, build, test, and refine data and visualization solutions Developing knowledge of Talent data, reporting processes, and business needs to identify opportunities and advise stakeholders The team The Talent Experience & Engagement People Analytics Team collaborates across Talent and the business to uncover data-driven insights to solve business challenges across the talent lifecycle and enable transformational change inside of Deloitte. Our teams (e.g., data science, surveys, candidate assessments, evaluation, strategic reporting, talent dashboards and storytelling) provide a holistic and client-centric approach that complements our wide range of analytical tools and methods to identify valuable workforce insights which, in turn, fuels the business. This role engages directly with Talent leadership/teams to deliver on requests across products and services People Analytics offers. We’re looking for a versatile professional to be a key player in our journey to deliver holistic data driven insights to our Talent leaders and teams. Location: Hyderabad Shift Timings: 2 PM to 11 PM Qualifications Required: Bachelor’s degree or postgraduate degree in Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Management, Statistics, Data Analytics, or a similar quantitative field 3-5 years of experience in reporting, analytics, or data visualization Experience using data transformation or data modeling tools such as Tableau Prep or Structured Query Language (SQL) Experience creating dashboards, visualizations, or storyboarded presentations for business stakeholders Experience using Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, Word, and Teams to analyze data, develop presentations, create written communications, and collaborate on deliverables Experience supporting stakeholder requests by translating business needs into reporting, analysis, or visualization outputs Preferred: Experience working with human resources or talent data Experience publishing and delivering content using Tableau Desktop or Tableau Server Experience using Databricks, including Databricks SQL or notebooks, to build or maintain data pipelines and curated datasets for analytics and reporting

title: USI - EH27- L45 - Senior tax services - Net Developers- Digital Enablement - Hyderabad
description: Deloitte Tax Services India Private LimitedDeloitte Tax Services India Private Limited commenced operations in January 2002. Since then, nearly all the Deloitte Tax LLP (“Deloitte Tax”) U.S. service lines and regions have developed their affiliations in India. Deloitte Tax offers you immense opportunities to learn and practice U.S. taxation, a much sought-after career option.At Deloitte, we are leading clients through the tax transformation taking place in the marketplace.  We offer a broad range of fully integrated tax services and add greater impact to clients by combining technology and tax technical resources to uncover insights and smarter solutions for navigating an increasingly complex global environment.DIGITAL ENABLEMENT OFFERING (Indirect Digital Enablement Center of Excellence) is a rapidly expanding competency at Deloitte. Deloitte is currently in the process of aggressively increasing its market share through our expansion of emerging and advanced technologies and look to hire talented individuals with qualifications to lead efforts through these technologies. This offering is designed towards our Analytics Services competency and focus area where we deliver Advanced Analytics and Data Visualization services.Job description for U.S. taxation: Function Deloitte Tax Services India Private Limited Service line Tax – Multistate (MTS) Job level Tax Consultant II or Tax Senior (Senior .Net Developer) Qualifications: Required: ·       .Net Core, ·       .Net Core API ·       SQL Server ·       Azure Technologies: ·       Azure SQL ·       Table Storage ·       Azure Functions ·       Design Patterns (DRY, SQRS) ·       Big volumes processing and performance improvement ·       High aptitude to actively learn new and emerging technologies Preferred: ·       Azure Devops ·       Git ·       UI UX Enthusiastic -Angular and React is a plus ·       Python ·       Azure Open AI Position Needed - Looking for qualified candidates with more than 5 years of experience with the technology stack, specially all related to serverless and Azure Cloud technologies. The candidate should have strong logical thinking skills and advanced analytics capabilities. Ideally, this individual should also be able to design data models and write complex sql scripts. Work you’ll do:·   Develop and maintain features for an existing application and feel comfortable with Green Field type of projects.·   Become a part of the Scrum team and work closely with BA, Dev, Scrum Master.·   Analyze functional requirements/PBIs and identify gaps in those items·   Write detailed documentation to a level of detail where your peers can easily follow.·   Work with BA and end users to perform UAT and deployment smoke tests for various environments.·   Be able to review the code of your peers and to apply the best practices and patterns to ensure code quality with a performance improvement mindset.·   Write Unit tests to guaranty a correct code coverage of all the features you and your team will be working on. ·   Write SQL queries with a strong sense of the impact in all the different environments.·   Coach junior team members by providing technical and business knowledge guidance.·   Be active in communications with the business team to collaborate through a project lifecycle as requirement changes occur, understanding the impacts that may have on the existing applications.·   Draft technical procedures and documentation for the applications including operations, user guide, etc.·   Help with ad hoc troubleshooting efforts through business process and production incidents.·   Technical expertise and aptitude to raise awareness of new and emerging technologies.Experience with:·   Excellent written and verbal communication skills at business and technical level of the organization·   Ability to work in a complex deadline-oriented environment and multi-task on multiple projects concurrently with little supervision.·   Ability to think and apply patterns from your and your peers’ code and DRY (Don’t Repeat Yourself).·   Table Storage and non-relational databases experience, table creation and the performance and cost toll it can get in a big volumes environment.    ·   SQL experience and advanced database design, querying, stored procedures, views, joins, performance management, etc.·   Ability to work with multi-threading and concurrent processes and the impact on the subsequent results. ·                     Ability to think analytically, be a problem solver and determine the interrelationships between solutions and requirementsPreferred Experience:·   C# ·   .Net Core (API)·   Azure (Table Storage, Functions, PaaS, Devops)·   Visual Studio and VS Code·   Git·   Advanced SQL (T-SQL, NOSQL)·   UI Technologies (Angular)Work Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 11 AM to 08:00 PM ISTHow you’ll growAt Deloitte, we’ve invested a great deal to create a rich environment in which our professionals can grow. We want all our people to develop in their own way, playing to their own strengths as they hone their leadership skills. And, as a part of our efforts, we provide our professionals with a variety of learning and networking opportunities—including exposure to leaders, sponsors, coaches, and challenging assignments—to help accelerate their careers along the way. No two people learn in exact same way. So, we provide a range of resources including live classrooms, team-based learning, and eLearning. title: USI-EH27-Tax-Global Employer Services(CTDT)- Business Analyst - L35 - TC II-Hyderabad
description: Deloitte’s Global Employer Solutions (GES) Client Technology Delivery Team is seeking an experienced business analyst to help deliver Deloitte’s GES technology enabled mobility services to meet Global Client business objectives.You will be part of our product delivery team, supporting several different delivery capabilities. You will work alongside other business analysts, testers, developers and business team members and report directly to the Product Owner.Your main tasks will include performing detailed requirements analysis, documenting processes, performing some user acceptance testing and identifying improvement opportunities.Work you'll doAs a Tax Technology Services Consultant II on the Global Employer Solutions Client Technology Delivery Team, you will be responsible for supporting technology-enabled mobility solutions and business analysis activities across the product delivery lifecycle. Gather, analyze, and document business and functional requirements in partnership with product owners, subject matter specialists, and stakeholders. Translate business needs into user stories, acceptance criteria, process flows, and other delivery artifacts for development and testing teams. Support requirements management across the delivery lifecycle, including scope changes, test case review, user acceptance testing, rollout support, training, and communications. Evaluate existing processes, identify improvement opportunities, and recommend changes to automate and modernize delivery practices. Facilitate stakeholder discussions, resolve requirement ambiguity, and communicate findings, priorities, and recommendations to cross-functional teams. The teamThe Global Employer Solutions Client Technology Delivery Team supports technology-enabled mobility services designed to help Deloitte deliver solutions aligned to client business objectives. The team works across product, business, and technology functions to translate complex needs into scalable delivery outcomes. Team members collaborate closely with product owners, developers, testers, and business stakeholders to drive quality, consistency, and continuous improvement.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 11 AM to 8 PM / 2 PM to 11 PMQualificationsRequired: 3+ years of experience as a business analyst supporting technology or product delivery initiatives Experience with Agile Scrum methodologies Experience eliciting, documenting, and managing business requirements through the development lifecycle Experience creating process documentation, process flows, user stories, and acceptance criteria Experience supporting user acceptance testing and test case review Experience working with cross-functional teams that include product, business, and technology stakeholders Preferred: Experience supporting tax technology, mobility technology, or global employer services solutions Experience facilitating stakeholder meetings, workshops, or requirement sessions Experience using agile delivery artifacts such as epics, features, and stories Experience supporting rollout activities, training, or change requests Experience using journey maps, empathy maps, or story maps#CA-GSD #CA-HPN

title: USI-EH27-Firm Tax-L25-Transfer Pricing
description: Analyst, Direct Tax – USI Firm Tax – Transfer PricingWe are looking for professionals with sound working knowledge in Indian transfer pricing regulations and international tax law matters. Work you’ll do  The primary area of focus for this position is ensuring transfer pricing compliances (amongst other corporate tax compliances as well), supporting with information for defending tax litigation for the group of entities (information gathering), supporting with working on APA and other advisory related work. Responsibilities include compilation /preparation of transfer pricing report, transfer pricing documentation and any other transfer pricing compliance forms.Key Responsibilities include: Compilation /preparation of transfer pricing report in Form 3CEB Co-management of preparation of TP Documentation including benchmarking study and any other transfer pricing compliance Preparing submissions/appeals/letters in relation to transfer pricing litigation, appearing before the tax authorities, preparation of supporting work papers for transfer pricing related matters Coordinating with Auditors for Annual Statutory Audit / Tax Audit and other Internal Audit requirements. Compiling periodic MIS reports pertaining to various Tax compliance metrics for appropriate analysis. Supervising and controlling tax compliance activities, coordinating for the payment of taxes with finance team members, acting as a liaison person between Finance and Firms Tax team. Be available as a contact person for external stakeholders and for liaising Revenue authorities, external Counsels, Tax advisors, etc. Assist with training and knowledge sharing within the Firm Tax Team. Create and/or update process documentation as processes change. Perform other job-related duties as assigned. The team  The USI Firms Tax team is responsible for ensuring entity level tax compliances for all the US India companies, besides catering to the tax essentials and advise to USI companies’ business and enabling functions ecosystems. The team consists of tax professionals with adequate expertise in handling tax matters. This team works in close coordination with the USI finance, legal and enabling besides business teams to ensure optimum tax health of the USI companies named above.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 11 AM to 8 PMQualificationsRequired: CA with 2 to 3 years’ experience with a large MNC or Big 4/consulting firm (preference would be a Big 4 or consulting firm) Basic understanding of transfer pricing provisions and regulations Knowledge about the transfer pricing compliances such as preparation of TP documentation, Form 3CEB and other compliance forms Knowledge about transfer pricing litigation on how to prepare response to TP notices (like show-cause notice, CIT(A) submissions, etc.) Ability to work independently and work together with a team in an energetic and dynamic work environment Proficiency in MS Office (including advanced Excel, Word and PowerPoint skills). A self-motivator, a team player cum individual contributor with knowledge of an ERP environment Good Communication, analytical skills, multi-tasking, and ability to work under pressure. Must be detail-oriented with a commitment to accuracy and quality work. Possess strong problem-solving skills with the ability to follow a transaction from beginning to end through various systems and reports.How you’ll grow At Deloitte, we’ve invested a great deal to create a rich environment in which our professionals can grow. We want all our people to develop in their own way, playing to their own strengths as they hone their leadership skills. And, as a part of our efforts, we provide our professionals with a variety of learning and networking opportunities— including exposure to leaders, sponsors, coaches, and challenging assignments—to help accelerate their careers along the way. No two people learn in exactly the same way. So, we provide a range of resources including live classrooms, team-based learning, and eLearning. DU: The Leadership Center in India, our state-of-the-art, worldclass learning Center in the Hyderabad offices is an extension of the Deloitte University (DU) in Westlake, Texas, and represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to our people’s growth and development. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India  Deloitte’s culture Our positive and supportive culture encourages our people to do their best work every day. We celebrate individuals by recognizing their uniqueness and offering them the flexibility to make daily choices that can help them to be healthy, centered, confident, and aware. We offer well-being programs and are continuously looking for new ways to maintain a culture that is inclusive, invites authenticity, leverages our diversity, and where our people excel and lead healthy, happy lives.  Learn more about Life at Deloitte.  Corporate citizenship Deloitte is led by a purpose: to make an impact that matters. We focus on education, giving, skill-based volunteerism, and leadership to help drive positive social impact in our communities.  Learn more about Deloitte’s impact on the world.
