title: USI-EH27-MF Canada-L25 Operations Analyst- Hyderabad
description: Operations Mandate – from an overall ITS perspective, leveraging USI resources is strategically aligning with the Firm’s Alternative Delivery Services model. Along with other professional lines of businesses such as Tax, Advisory, Consulting and Enabling Areas like Marketplace and Business Operations, ITS is building its services and capabilities in USI to conduct operational tasks and responsibilities that allow Canadian onshore teams to focus more on ITS and business priorities. In addition, ITS continues to grow and strengthen its Support Services presence in Hyderabad to provide quality client-facing support for our USI Canada Professionals.The Cloud Infra Operations function of USI DT CA Technology and Infrastructure group is accountable for application support/deployment/implementations, tuning, maintenance & problem solving, working closely with development teams for Enterprise Business Applications etc. This function is responsible for applications across the firm and works closely with other global teams.Work you'll doAs an Operations Analyst on the Cloud Infra DSO & Project Delivery Platforms team, you will be responsible for supporting enterprise applications and infrastructure across cloud and on-premises environments while helping maintain reliability, security, and operational efficiency.Provide day-to-day operational support for enterprise applications across Azure, AWS, and hybrid environments.Monitor application availability and performance, respond to incidents and service requests, and drive issues through resolution with appropriate escalation and communication.Perform deployments, patching, configuration updates, and release support using Azure DevOps, GitHub, and established DevSecOps and change management processes.Administer and troubleshoot Windows Server, Linux, IIS, SQL Server, SharePoint, and containerized platforms including AKS and EKS.Create and maintain runbooks, knowledge articles, disaster recovery documentation, and operational procedures to support governance, continuity, and knowledge transfer.The teamUSI DT Canada is the Canada CIO’s IT department which manages an end-to-end portfolio of Canada business applications and technology infrastructure that supports business processes common to Deloitte Canada member firm.Our IT & Systems team works behind the scenes and is essential to the Deloitte organization. Our forward-thinking team members have the analytical skills needed to parse mountains of data and stellar communications skills needed to present research, discovery, and recommendations in a logical and easily understandable way. Our technologists provide IT infrastructure support to develop custom enterprise applications.In addition, Technology and Infrastructure team manages datacenters, servers, applications, databases, operating systems, security and patching systems, network, etc including maintenance, routine operations, Infrastructure-related Event and Incident Management.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 11 AM to 8 PM / 2 PM to 11 PMQualificationsRequired:Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or equivalent work experience1-3 years of experience supporting Windows Server, Red Hat Enterprise Linux, Azure App Services, and SharePoint services within an Information Technology Infrastructure Library (ITIL)-based environmentExperience administering Microsoft Azure and Amazon Web ServicesExperience with Azure DevOps pipelines, release pipelines, or GitHub for application deploymentsExperience supporting Active Directory, Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) certificates, Domain Name System (DNS), and hybrid application hosting environmentsExperience with Docker, Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), or Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)Experience scripting in PowerShell, Bash, or PythonPreferred:ITIL v3 Foundation, Windows Server Administration, SharePoint or SharePoint Online, or Azure Fundamentals certificationExperience with Dynatrace or other observability platformsExperience with infrastructure as code using Azure Resource Manager (ARM) or AWS CloudFormationExperience supporting enterprise applications such as PeopleSoft, Salesforce, CRM, Cognos, or other ERP platformsExperience with AKS or EKS deployments, manifest updates, Helm charts, custom domain configuration, and SSL certificate configurationBroad infrastructure administration experience across servers, networks, databases, load balancers, SharePoint, SharePoint Online, Active Directory Federation Services (ADFS), and Active Directory

title: USI-EH27-Consulting Services-EP-SCNO-MES Aveva-Manager
description: MES PAS-X – Senior Consultant, Technical TransformationJoin Deloitte’s Enterprise Performance practice as a Senior Consultant, MES PASX, where you will support the delivery of manufacturing execution system solutions across complex manufacturing environments. In this role, you will contribute to MES design, configuration, implementation, integration, validation, and deployment activities that help clients improve operational performance, compliance, and digital manufacturing capabilities. You will work with cross-functional stakeholders to deliver quality work products, support business requirements, and help drive scalable technology solutions aligned to client objectives.Work you'll doAs a Senior Consultant, MES PASX on the Enterprise Performance team, you will be responsible for leading and supporting MES transformation and implementation initiatives across client environments. Lead and support requirements gathering, process analysis, solution design, configuration, testing, and deployment activities for Werum PAS-X implementations. Configure and extend MES capabilities across specification, execution, compliance, performance, master batch records, state diagrams, and master data. Lead workshops for requirements, data model validation, configuration validation, and solution design across business and technology stakeholders. Support MES integration activities across enterprise resource planning, automation, product lifecycle management, advanced planning systems, laboratory information management systems, and other connected platforms. Contribute to solution documentation, validation activities, training support, issue resolution, and continuous improvement across MES implementations and enhancement releases. The teamEnterprise PerformanceThe Enterprise Performance team leverages deep industry knowledge, strong analytical skills, and practical approaches to address clients’ toughest business challenges. Our professionals as part of the Supply Chain Network Operations (SCNO) service line within Enterprise Performance focus on helping organizations achieve sustainable competitive advantage throughout their operations, spanning product development, planning, sourcing, manufacturing, logistics, and distribution. We excel at translating strategic objectives into tangible, measurable outcomes at the operational level. By aligning high-level goals with frontline execution, we ensure our clients realize real value and improved performance across every stage of their supply chain and operations.Location: Bengaluru / Hyderabad / Pune / ChennaiShift Timings: 11 AM to 8 PM or 2 PM to 11 PM IST, as per business requirementsQualificationsRequired: Bachelor of Engineering, Bachelor of Technology, Master of Computer Applications, Master of Technology, or equivalent degree from an accredited university 5+ years of experience in manufacturing execution systems in large company environments Experience with Werum PAS-X, including Specification, Execution, Compliance, and Performance modules Experience with master batch record creation, state diagram design, and master data configuration Experience configuring, administering, or customizing PAS-X modules, including GMBRs, PVL, PMBRs, Equipment Management, Material Flow, Weigh and Dispense, Electronic Batch Records, and Master Data Management Experience applying ISA-95 or ISA-88 standards and GxP, GAMP, or 21 CFR Part 11 compliance requirements to MES design Experience integrating MES with SAP, enterprise resource planning systems, or Level 2 automation systems using Java, J2EE, C# .NET, Oracle, SQL, RESTful APIs, or web services Preferred: Experience leading requirements workshops, data model workshops, or configuration validation sessions Experience designing, prototyping, testing, training, or supporting MES implementations Experience conducting AS-IS assessments and developing TO-BE process or solution designs Experience with control systems, product lifecycle management, advanced planning systems, or laboratory information management systems Experience with Level 3 or Level 4 systems and BOM, MBOM, or BOP structures Experience with digital manufacturing, Industry 4.0, digital supply networks, or automation trends

title: USI - EH27 - Product Engineering HXM - SAP FIORI AND CAPM - Software Engineer III
description: SAP Fiori Consultant  (USI Deloitte Technology – Product Engineering)Professional Summary As a Senior Consultant at Deloitte Consulting, you will be responsible for delivering high-quality SAP solutions within defined timelines. You will work independently on development tasks, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and mentor junior team members when required. This role demands strong expertise in SAP BTP and Fiori/UI5, along with the ability to handle complex technical challenges and contribute to end-to-end project delivery. Key Responsibilities Outcome-Driven Accountability: Embrace and drive a culture of accountability for customer and business outcomes. Develop engineering solutions that solve complex problems with valuable outcomes, ensuring high-quality, lean designs and implementations. Technical Leadership and Advocacy: Serve as the technical advocate for products, ensuring architectural integrity, feasibility, and alignment with business and customer goals, NFRs, and applicable architecture and engineering standards—being responsible for product architecture blueprints, high-level architecture designs (e.g., “4+1 model” or relevant others), development/implementation of end-to-end AI/ML solutions, and integration architecture into the technical landscape and technology stack. Engineering Craftsmanship: Possess passion and experience as an individual contributor, responsible for the engineering designs and technical feasibility of solutions, being hands-on with design, configuration and code part of the time, contributing to team velocity. Actively get engaged with engineers to ensure architecture is understood and can be implemented, working with them closely during sprints, helping resolve any technical issues through to production operations: reviewing code, actively driving technology debt reduction, and helping drive engineering quality. Be self-driven to learn new technologies, experiment with engineers, and inspire the team to learn and drive application of those new technologies. Customer-Centric Engineering: Develop lean engineering solutions through rapid, inexpensive experimentation to solve customer needs. Engage with customers and product teams to deliver the right architectural solution and machine learning models for the product in the right way at the right time. Incremental and Iterative Delivery: Exhibit a mindset that favors action and evidence over extensive planning. Utilize a leaning-forward approach to navigate complexity and uncertainty, delivering lean, supportable, and maintainable solutions. Cross-Functional Collaboration and Integration: Work collaboratively with empowered, cross-functional teams including product management, experience, delivery, infrastructure, and security. Integrate diverse perspectives to make well-informed decisions that balance feasibility, viability, usability, and value. Foster a collaborative environment that enhances team synergy and innovation. Advanced Technical Proficiency: Possess deep expertise in modern software engineering practices and principles, deep learning and Agentic AI solutions, including OOD/OOP, Agile methodologies, MLOps/AgentOps, DevSecOps, Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment, deployment techniques like Blue-Green, Canary to minimize down-time and enable A/B testing approaches. Act as a Role-Model, leveraging these techniques to optimize solutioning and product delivery, ensuring high-quality outcomes with minimal waste. Demonstrate proficiency in product development, from conceptualization and design to implementation and scaling, with a focus on continuous improvement and learning. Domain Expertise: Quickly acquire domain-specific knowledge relevant to the business or product. Translate business/user needs into technical requirements, designs, and robust data processing pipelines. Navigate various enterprise functions such as business and enabling areas as well as product, experience, delivery, infrastructure, and security to drive product value and feasibility as well as alignment with organizational goals. Effective Communication and Influence: Exhibit exceptional communication skills, capable of articulating complex technical concepts clearly and compellingly. Inspire and influence stakeholders at all levels through well-structured arguments and trade-offs supported by evidence, evaluations, and research. Create coherent narratives that align technical solutions with business objectives. Engagement and Collaborative Co-Creation: Engage and collaborate with stakeholders at all organizational levels, from team members to senior executives. Build and maintain constructive relationships, fostering a culture of co-creation and shared momentum towards achieving product goals. Align diverse perspectives and drive consensus to create feasible solutions. The team: US Deloitte Technology Product Engineering has modernized software and product delivery, creating a scalable, cost-effective model that focuses on value/outcomes that leverages a progressive and responsive talent structure. As Deloitte’s primary internal development team, Product Engineering delivers innovative digital solutions to businesses, service lines, and internal operations with proven bottom-line results and outcomes. It helps power Deloitte’s success. It is the engine that drives Deloitte, serving many of the world’s largest, most respected companies. We develop and deploy cutting-edge internal and go-to-market solutions that help Deloitte operate effectively and lead in the market. Our reputation is built on a tradition of delivering with excellence. Roles and Responsibilties: • Build Custom SAP Fiori / UI5 applications using JavaScript, HTML5, CSS3 and MVC architecture • Design, develop, and deliver high-quality code and technical objects using SAP BTP and Fiori/UI5 • Extend and enhance standard SAP Fiori applications using SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) or Web IDE • Develop and consume OData services to connect front -end applications with SAP SF using CI or API and SAP backend systems (ECC or S/4 HANA) • Configure Fiori Launchpad catalogs, pages, groups, roles and tiles. • Implement Fiori Design Guidelines to ensure intuitive, user-centric and responsive interfaces. • Understand business requirements and translate them into scalable technical solutions • Work closely with functional teams to clarify requirements and resolve issues • Develop and maintain technical documentation • Perform unit testing and optimize application performance • Participate in all project phases including design, development, testing, and hyper-care support • Ensure code quality through reviews and adherence to best practices • Identify improvement opportunities and propose sustainable solutions • Collaborate with onsite and offshore teams • Mentor junior team members and support team capability development • Deliver assigned tasks within defined timelines Required Skills & Experience • 6+ years of experience in SAP development and implementation projects • Hands-on experience with SAP BAS and CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) • Experience in at least 2–3 end-to-end implementation projects • Strong knowledge of: ? CAP model development (API consumption, HANA DB integration, service deployment) ? SAP BTP debugging and troubleshooting ? Integration and connectivity (BTP, S/4HANA, CPI/CI, SuccessFactors basics) ? OAuth 2.0 concepts (JWT, XSUAA, Approuter) • Experience in developing FIORI applications (Freestyle SAPUI5) • Experience with deployment on Cloud Foundry • Hands-on experience with SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) • Experience with SAP Build Work Zone configuration and Git Version Control • Knowledge on Smart Controls and annotation-based development • Ability to understand and implement technical design documents • Strong analytical problem-solving, collaboration and communication skills Good to Have • Experience in SAP Integration, ABAP, or SuccessFactors • Exposure to additional SAP modules or technologies Education • BE / B.Tech / MCA / M.Sc (Computer Science) or equivalent from a recognized university How You will Grow: At Deloitte, our professional development plans focus on helping people at every level of their career to identify and use their strengths to do their best work every day and excel in everything they do.

Develop engineering solutions that solve complex problems with valuable outcomes, ensuring high-quality, lean designs and implementations. Technical Leadership and Advocacy: Serve as the technical advocate for products, ensuring architectural integrity, feasibility, and alignment with business and customer goals, NFRs, and applicable architecture and engineering standards—being responsible for product architecture blueprints, high-level architecture designs (e.g., “4+1 model” or relevant others), development/implementation of end-to-end AI/ML solutions, and integration architecture into the technical landscape and technology stack. Engineering Craftsmanship: Possess passion and experience as an individual contributor, responsible for the engineering designs and technical feasibility of solutions, being hands-on with design, configuration and code part of the time, contributing to team velocity. Actively get engaged with engineers to ensure architecture is understood and can be implemented, working with them closely during sprints, helping resolve any technical issues through to production operations: reviewing code, actively driving technology debt reduction, and helping drive engineering quality. Be self-driven to learn new technologies, experiment with engineers, and inspire the team to learn and drive application of those new technologies. Customer-Centric Engineering: Develop lean engineering solutions through rapid, inexpensive experimentation to solve customer needs. Engage with customers and product teams to deliver the right architectural solution and machine learning models for the product in the right way at the right time. Incremental and Iterative Delivery: Exhibit a mindset that favors action and evidence over extensive planning. Utilize a leaning-forward approach to navigate complexity and uncertainty, delivering lean, supportable, and maintainable solutions. Cross-Functional Collaboration and Integration: Work collaboratively with empowered, cross-functional teams including product management, experience, delivery, infrastructure, and security. Integrate diverse perspectives to make well-informed decisions that balance feasibility, viability, usability, and value. Foster a collaborative environment that enhances team synergy and innovation. Advanced Technical Proficiency: Possess deep expertise in modern software engineering practices and principles, deep learning and Agentic AI solutions, including OOD/OOP, Agile methodologies, MLOps/AgentOps, DevSecOps, Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment, deployment techniques like Blue-Green, Canary to minimize down-time and enable A/B testing approaches. Act as a Role-Model, leveraging these techniques to optimize solutioning and product delivery, ensuring high-quality outcomes with minimal waste. Demonstrate proficiency in product development, from conceptualization and design to implementation and scaling, with a focus on continuous improvement and learning. Domain Expertise: Quickly acquire domain-specific knowledge relevant to the business or product. Translate business/user needs into technical requirements, designs, and robust data processing pipelines. Navigate various enterprise functions such as business and enabling areas as well as product, experience, delivery, infrastructure, and security to drive product value and feasibility as well as alignment with organizational goals. Effective Communication and Influence: Exhibit exceptional communication skills, capable of articulating complex technical concepts clearly and compellingly. Inspire and influence stakeholders at all levels through well-structured arguments and trade-offs supported by evidence, evaluations, and research. Create coherent narratives that align technical solutions with business objectives. Engagement and Collaborative Co-Creation: Engage and collaborate with stakeholders at all organizational levels, from team members to senior executives. Build and maintain constructive relationships, fostering a culture of co-creation and shared momentum towards achieving product goals. Align diverse perspectives and drive consensus to create feasible solutions. The team: US Deloitte Technology Product Engineering has modernized software and product delivery, creating a scalable, cost-effective model that focuses on value/outcomes that leverages a progressive and responsive talent structure. As Deloitte’s primary internal development team, Product Engineering delivers innovative digital solutions to businesses, service lines, and internal operations with proven bottom-line results and outcomes. It helps power Deloitte’s success. It is the engine that drives Deloitte, serving many of the world’s largest, most respected companies. We develop and deploy cutting-edge internal and go-to-market solutions that help Deloitte operate effectively and lead in the market. Our reputation is built on a tradition of delivering with excellence. Roles and Responsibilties: • Build Custom SAP Fiori / UI5 applications using JavaScript, HTML5, CSS3 and MVC architecture • Design, develop, and deliver high-quality code and technical objects using SAP BTP and Fiori/UI5 • Extend and enhance standard SAP Fiori applications using SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) or Web IDE • Develop and consume OData services to connect front -end applications with SAP SF using CI or API and SAP backend systems (ECC or S/4 HANA) • Configure Fiori Launchpad catalogs, pages, groups, roles and tiles. • Implement Fiori Design Guidelines to ensure intuitive, user-centric and responsive interfaces. • Understand business requirements and translate them into scalable technical solutions • Work closely with functional teams to clarify requirements and resolve issues • Develop and maintain technical documentation • Perform unit testing and optimize application performance • Participate in all project phases including design, development, testing, and hyper-care support • Ensure code quality through reviews and adherence to best practices • Identify improvement opportunities and propose sustainable solutions • Collaborate with onsite and offshore teams • Mentor junior team members and support team capability development • Deliver assigned tasks within defined timelines Required Skills & Experience • 6+ years of experience in SAP development and implementation projects • Hands-on experience with SAP BAS and CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) • Experience in at least 2–3 end-to-end implementation projects • Strong knowledge of: ? CAP model development (API consumption, HANA DB integration, service deployment) ? SAP BTP debugging and troubleshooting ? Integration and connectivity (BTP, S/4HANA, CPI/CI, SuccessFactors basics) ? OAuth 2.0 concepts (JWT, XSUAA, Approuter) • Experience in developing FIORI applications (Freestyle SAPUI5) • Experience with deployment on Cloud Foundry • Hands-on experience with SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) • Experience with SAP Build Work Zone configuration and Git Version Control • Knowledge on Smart Controls and annotation-based development • Ability to understand and implement technical design documents • Strong analytical problem-solving, collaboration and communication skills Good to Have • Experience in SAP Integration, ABAP, or SuccessFactors • Exposure to additional SAP modules or technologies Education • BE / B.Tech / MCA / M.Sc (Computer Science) or equivalent from a recognized university How You will Grow: At Deloitte, our professional development plans focus on helping people at every level of their career to identify and use their strengths to do their best work every day and excel in everything they do.

title: USI - EH27 - EOX - RES - Workplace Experience & Design (WED) operations - Senior Analyst - Hyderabad
description: The Workplace Experience & Design (WED) Operations role supports Deloitte workplaces by enabling the systems, tools, and processes that drive workplace measurement, planning, and execution.Working in close partnership with the U.S. Real Estate Workplace Experience & Design teams, this role translates strategy, measurement insights, and operational needs into structured workflows, tools, and actionable plans that support workplace strategy and delivery.Sitting at the intersection of analytics, operations, workplace strategy, and technology, this role partners across functions to ensure WED initiatives are data-driven, well-governed, and effectively executed—not just well-designed.Work you'll doAs a Specialist, Analytics and Insights on the Workplace Experience & Design team, you will be responsible for:• Translate WED and Real Estate strategy into structured workstreams, execution plans, and roadmap delivery activities.• Lead measurement and analytics enablement through dashboards, tools, key performance indicators, and reporting that support data-driven decisions.• Support strategic planning, governance, and standards tracking across workplace initiatives, including forecasting, risk identification, and compliance reporting.• Improve workflows, tools, and operating processes to increase efficiency, transparency, scalability, and adoption across teams.• Coordinate execution across Workplace Experience & Design, Real Estate, WIQ, and Technology stakeholders to manage dependencies and deliver initiatives.The teamThe Deloitte Real Estate function is an integrated, cross-functional team spanning strategy, workplace experience, design, operations, and delivery. This role will be based in Hyderabad as part of the USI Real Estate / Workplace Experience capability and will work directly with U.S. leadership to support a global workplace portfolio.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 2 PM to 11 PMQualificationsRequired:• 5-10 years of experience in workplace strategy, workplace analytics, workplace transformation, or program management.• Bachelor’s degree in architecture, engineering, business, analytics, or a related field.• Experience with data visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau and advanced Microsoft Excel.• Experience with workflow or project management tools such as Smartsheet or similar platforms.• Experience developing metrics, dashboards, or reporting to support planning and decision[1]making.• Experience managing multiple workstreams, project plans, or operational activities in a structured environment.• Experience working with cross-functional stakeholders in a global environment.Preferred:• Background in architecture, workplace design, environmental design, or workplace consulting.• Familiarity with LEED, WELL, or sustainability and wellness concepts in workplace environments.• Experience facilitating workshops or structured planning sessions.• Familiarity with collaboration tools such as Mural or Miro.• Experience in process improvement, tool implementation, or workflow design.A couple of review notes:• I tightened the qualifications for measurability and candidate clarity.• The derived posting title Specialist, Analytics and Insights is compliant with the intake format, but it may not be candidate-friendly if used on a posting

title: USI-EH27-Consulting Services-EP-SCNO-Critical Manufacturing-Senior Consultant
description: Critical Manufacturing – Senior Consultant, Technical TransformationDeloitte is seeking a Senior Consultant, Technical Transformation to support Manufacturing Execution System (MES) transformation initiatives within its Supply Chain & Network Operations practice. In this role, you will help clients design, implement, and optimize Critical Manufacturing MES solutions to improve operational performance across complex manufacturing environments. The ideal candidate brings strong technical and functional experience across MES architecture, integrations, and deployment, along with the ability to translate business needs into scalable digital manufacturing solutions.Work you'll doAs a Senior Consultant, Technical Transformation on the Enterprise Performance team, you will be responsible for supporting clients through the design, implementation, and optimization of Critical Manufacturing MES solutions across manufacturing operations. Lead and support Critical Manufacturing MES implementations, including requirements gathering, functional design, prototyping, configuration, testing, training, and deployment. Serve as a technical and solution architect for manufacturing transformation initiatives spanning MES, plant systems, and enterprise integrations. Conduct client workshops to validate requirements, define solution design, and document future-state MES processes and data models. Design and develop integrations between Critical Manufacturing MES and enterprise or plant systems, including SAP, product lifecycle management, advanced planning systems, laboratory information management systems, and automation platforms. Collaborate with client stakeholders, plant users, and subject matter specialists to assess current-state environments and define scalable future-state solutions. Support practice growth through proposal development, thought leadership, and innovation in digital manufacturing and Industry 4.0-enabled MES offerings. The teamEnterprise PerformanceThe Enterprise Performance team leverages deep industry knowledge, strong analytical skills, and practical approaches to address clients’ toughest business challenges. Our professionals as part of the Supply Chain Network Operations (SCNO) service line within Enterprise Performance focus on helping organizations achieve sustainable competitive advantage throughout their operations, spanning product development, planning, sourcing, manufacturing, logistics, and distribution. We excel at translating strategic objectives into tangible, measurable outcomes at the operational level. By aligning high-level goals with frontline execution, we ensure our clients realize real value and improved performance across every stage of their supply chain and operations.Location: Bengaluru / Hyderabad / Pune / ChennaiShift Timings: 11 AM to 8 PM or 2 PM to 11 PM IST, as per business requirementsQualificationsRequired: 6-9 years of experience with the Critical Manufacturing MES platform, including configuration, customization, and deployment in discrete manufacturing environments Experience using Critical Manufacturing MES DEE for custom development and Critical Manufacturing MES Connect for integrations Experience designing or supporting MES integrations using Hypertext Transfer Protocol application programming interfaces, web services, SECS/GEM, or OPC-UA Experience with C# .NET, JavaScript or TypeScript, SQL Server, and SQL stored procedures Knowledge of ISA-95 or ISA-88 standards and regulated manufacturing requirements, including GxP, GAMP, or 21 CFR Part 11 Experience working with MES functional areas such as quality management, work-in-process tracking, dispatch management, scheduling, genealogy or traceability, or maintenance management Ability to travel 20-40%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sectors you serve. Preferred: Experience with cloud or Internet of Things-enabled MES reporting and intelligence solutions, including Azure or Amazon Web Services Experience with Mendix or other low-code platforms used in manufacturing environments Experience integrating MES with enterprise resource planning, product lifecycle management, laboratory information management systems, or advanced planning systems Experience in semiconductor, electronics, or discrete manufacturing environments Bachelor of Technology or Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science, Electronics, Mechanical Engineering, or another engineering discipline Master of Computer Applications, Master of Technology, or Master of Science

title: USI-EH27-GPS (G&P National)-L35-Services Specialist- Market Business Relationship (EPM)- Hyderabad
description: Services Specialist, Firm Risk, Compliance, and Governance Services- Market Business RelationshipDeloitte’s Enterprise Portfolio Management (EPM) team plays a crucial and growing role in helping the firm achieve its Multi-Disciplinary Model (MDM) objectives. EPM is seeking a high performing Analyst for a highly visible team working with senior business, industry, and account leaders to help modernize the Firm’s management of its third-party relationships to support critical MDM agility. The position sits in Growth & Purpose as part of the EPM team and is part of the larger Office of MDM that reports to the Chief Growth & Purpose Officer and business CEOs. The role offers a unique opportunity to help operationalize a new capability aimed at enabling Deloitte’s three world-class businesses to thrive.Work you'll doAs a Services Specialist, Firm Risk, Compliance, and Governance Services on the Enterprise Portfolio Management (EPM) team, you will be responsible for: Managing third-party risk data and processes, including data entry, verification, maintenance, audits, renewals, and closure of third-party engagement records in Deloitte compliance systems Supporting implementation of the MBR Modernization initiative to improve visibility into Deloitte’s sell-with relationships and support growth Activating the MBR operating model by gathering relationship information, confirming segmentation, identifying relationship owners, engaging LCSPs, and ensuring appropriate signaling in internal systems Engaging with stakeholders across the firm to support third-party relationships through the lifecycle, from early-stage discussions through establishment of strategic relationships Developing recurring reporting, dashboards, and status summaries for leadership on volumes, aging, segmentation, renewal status, and operational performance Supporting governance, quality control, process standardization, and issue resolution across Risk, Independence, and business relationship teams The teamEPM is a “heart of the business” multi-function, integrated team that brings deep MDM insights to tackle some of the firm’s most complex challenges. EPM’s mandate is to optimize our business footprint and unlock revenue by effectively navigating cross-business conflicts and Independence constraints while reducing MDM tension. Optimization requires EPM to proactively inform our business leaders on MDM implications for strategy and investment considerations, as well as vision to see beyond the firm’s current state to influence and optimize for our business of tomorrow.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 2 PM to 11 PMQualificationsRequired: 3+ years of professional experience in consulting, client service, project management, program management, compliance operations, governance operations, or data management Experience maintaining records and data in business systems or compliance platforms Experience performing data entry, data verification, reporting, audits, and record remediation activities Experience developing dashboards, status reporting, or operational summaries for leadership Experience working with cross-functional stakeholders in a virtual team environment Strong English oral and written communication skills Ability to work the 2 PM to 11 PM shift Preferred: Experience supporting third-party risk, independence, compliance, governance, or marketplace business relationship processes Experience working with internal business systems and tools Experience supporting process improvement, playbook development, or operating procedure standardization Experience partnering with Risk, Independence, or business relationship stakeholders Experience working in ambiguous or evolving business environments

title: USI FY27 | Cyber Operate- Wiz.io - Senior Consultant
description: Deloitte Cyber understands the unique challenges and opportunities businesses face in cybersecurity. Join our team to deliver powerful insights to help our clients navigate the ever-changing threat design, and technology as we partner with clients to transform finance. Work you'll do As a Senior Consultant on the Security Platform team, you will be responsible for supporting clients in securing cloud environments using Wiz. Advise clients on Wiz capabilities, including Wiz Compute, Data Security, Threat Detection and Response, Code Scanning, Software as a Service (SaaS), and Container Security. Support client transitions to Wiz, including migration, integration, and optimization of security controls and processes. Evaluate cloud services against standards such as Center for Internet Security (CIS), National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), and General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), and assess environment coverage within Wiz. Monitor vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and compliance issues, and deliver cloud security reports with actionable recommendations. Support policy development, incident response, data protection, access control, resource configuration activities, and documentation of technical issues and resolutions. The team Security Platform teams embed security in all aspects of digital transformation by securing a client’s “technical backbone” while also enabling a secure platform for the cloud environment. Services include security architecture, secure development and deployment, end-to-end cyber cloud capabilities, application security, and security for emerging technologies and connected products. Examples of work include Secure by Design, Cloud Security Orchestration & Automation, Core Infrastructure Security, and Secure Software Enablement. Deloitte Cyber team helps complex organizations more confidently pursue their growth, innovation and performance agendas through proactive management of the associated cyber risks. Our professionals provide advisory and implementation services that integrate risk, regulatory, and technology skills to help clients transform their legacy programs into proactive Secure.Vigilant.Resilient.TM cyber programs. Cloud Infrastructure supports organizations through the complete cycle of migrating business processes to the cloud, with capabilities and solutions focused on development, transformation, and resilience of cloud security. Location: Bengaluru / Hyderabad / Pune / Chennai Shift Timings: 24*7 rotational shifts; flexibility for night, weekend, and holiday coverage is essential; on-call support required based on project assignment Qualifications Required: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Cyber Security, Information Security, Engineering, Information Technology, or another technical field 5+ years of experience in technical consulting, including architecting and designing security solutions 4+ years of experience implementing security posture models using Wiz.io Experience developing code or policy using REGO, Open Policy Agent, Terraform, Python, or CloudFormation Experience with Cloud Security Posture Management and Cloud Workload Protection Platform capabilities Experience working across Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud Platform environments Preferred: Certified Cloud Security Professional or Certificate of Cloud Security Knowledge AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner, Microsoft Certified: Azure Fundamentals, Google Cloud Digital Leader, or another cloud or security certification#Cyber_Cyber Operate

title: USI-EH27-Consulting-FT-BF-Hyperion P&E-Senior Consultant, Functional Transformation
description: Finance TransformationAs a Senior Consultant at Deloitte Consulting, you will lead the design, build, and delivery of Oracle Cloud FP&A implementations, working with global teams to turn requirements into deliverables. You will guide junior team members and manage project phases to help deliver timely, high-quality solutions.Work you'll doAs a Senior Consultant, Functional Transformation on the Business Finance team, you will be responsible for:Leading the design, development, and deployment of Hyperion Planning, Essbase, Planning and Budgeting Cloud Service (PBCS), Enterprise Planning and Budgeting Cloud Service (EPBCS), and Profitability and Cost Management Cloud Service (PCMCS) applicationsGathering requirements and supporting delivery activities across Oracle Enterprise Performance Management (EPM) engagementsDeveloping integrations, calculation scripts, business rules, dashboards, forms, and reports to support planning and performance management processesManaging testing, user acceptance testing, hypercare, issue resolution, and application performance improvement activitiesCollaborating with business stakeholders, technical teams, and junior practitioners to support solution delivery and team capability developmentThe teamBusiness Finance, part of our Finance Transformation portfolio, delivers solution-as-a-service offerings focused on digital finance strategies, financial planning and analysis, and enterprise performance technologies. We take a holistic approach, integrating strategy development, process design, and technology enablement to help clients overcome challenges and achieve sustained business success. Our team partners with organizations to drive profitable growth, manage costs strategically, and navigate financial risks. We are seeking a highly motivated individual with strong analytical skills and business acumen to join us in delivering innovative solutions and supporting clients in their transformation journey.Location: Bengaluru/Hyderabad/Pune/ChennaiQualificationsRequired:CA, BE, or B.Tech degree6+ years of experience with Oracle PlanningExperience with Enterprise Planning and Budgeting Cloud Service (EPBCS) or Profitability and Cost Management Cloud Service (PCMCS), including out-of-the-box modules, custom cubes, or reporting cubesExperience leading delivery across Oracle Enterprise Performance Management (EPM) Suite projectsExperience with business rules, forms, dashboards, data integration, metadata management, and MDX calculationsExperience with EPM Automate, batch or shell operations, Groovy scripting, and Shared Services user provisioning and security setupExperience supporting financial planning, budgeting, and forecasting processesPreferred:Experience with prototyping, solution enhancement, and performance optimization in Oracle EPM applicationsExperience with Oracle Data Integrator (ODI), Enterprise Data Management Cloud Service (EDMCS), Enterprise Performance Reporting Cloud Service (EPRCS), or third-party reporting tools#FY27_FT_BF   #FinanceTransformation   #FY27ReferralCampaign

title: USI- EH27-DTTL-DT GP&S- Solution Architect(SAP)
description: Deloitte Technology develops and deploys internal and go-to-market solutions that help Deloitte operate effectively and innovate at scale. As a Lead Architect, you will shape SAP architecture decisions across ABAP and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) landscapes, with a focus on clean core, extensibility, and enterprise delivery. This role requires deep experience in SAP architecture, modernization, integration, and platform governance. You will work with technical and business stakeholders to design scalable solutions that support Deloitte’s technology priorities.Work you'll doAs a Lead Architect on the Deloitte Technology team, you will be responsible for… Define target architecture for ABAP and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) solutions, including in-app, side-by-side, and hybrid extension patterns aligned to clean core principles Lead solution design using SAP BTP services, including Cloud Foundry, Kyma, Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), HANA Cloud, Integration Suite, API Management, Extension Suite, Workflow, and Mobile Services Drive modernization of ABAP assets using object-oriented ABAP, RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), and Core Data Services (CDS), including guidance on refactoring and reimplementation approaches Design integration, security, and governance patterns across hybrid SAP landscapes, including application programming interfaces (APIs), authentication, authorization, monitoring, and data protection Lead architecture workshops, proof of concepts, and delivery enablement activities, including architecture decision records, DevOps strategy, and release governance for ABAP and BTP artifacts The teamDeloitte Technology helps power Deloitte’s success, which serves many of the world’s largest, most respected organizations. We develop and deploy cutting-edge internal and go-to-market solutions that help Deloitte operate effectively and lead in the market. Our reputation is built on a tradition of delivering with excellence.The internal software and applications development team is responsible for delivering leading-edge technologies to Deloitte professionals. Their broad portfolio includes web and mobile productivity tools that empower our people to log expenses, enter timesheets, book travel and more, anywhere, anytime. DAS enables our client service professionals through a comprehensive suite of applications across the business lines. In addition to application delivery, DAS offers full-scale design services, a robust mobile portfolio, cutting-edge analytics, and innovative custom development.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 11 AM to 8 PMQualificationsRequired: 8+ years of experience in SAP development 3-5 years of experience in architecture or technical lead roles Experience with object-oriented ABAP, RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), Core Data Services (CDS), and S/4HANA custom code remediation Experience designing and delivering solutions on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), including Cloud Foundry, Kyma, CAP, HANA Cloud, Integration Suite, API Management, and Extension Suite Experience designing integration patterns using OData, Representational State Transfer (REST), Simple Object Access Protocol (SOAP), and Intermediate Document (IDoc), where applicable Experience defining security and governance approaches across hybrid SAP landscapes, including OAuth 2.0, SAML, and XSUAA Experience with Git, continuous integration/continuous deployment pipelines, automated testing, and release orchestration for SAP applications Preferred: SAP certifications in ABAP, S/4HANA, or SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Experience with SAP Cloud SDK, UI5, Fiori, Node.js, Java microservices, and observability tooling Experience supporting SAP Joule pilots or SAP Build governance programs

title: USI FY27 | OSH - Cyber Operate - Pre Sales - Manager
description: As a Manager at Deloitte Consulting, you’ll work as Cyber Solution Architect within the Operate Solution Hub, you will be responsible for coordinating the design of large and complex managed cybersecurity deals. These deals require solutioning for multi-domain within Cyber such as Infrastructure Security, IAM, GRC, Data Security, Vulnerability Management. The Solution Hub is designed to drive an optimum process for solutioning and pricing cross-cyber-domain managed security deals, reducing friction for cross-Offering Portfolio pursuits and optimizing review. By providing services such as inputs for deal qualifications, cyber benchmarks/effort estimation, and solution/pricing review, the Cyber Operate Solution Hub will be a focused space to increase win rates and profitability. Work you'll do As a Manager on the Cyber Operate Solution Hub team, you will be responsible for solution design and architecture activities: Leading development of cyber solution storyboards, total cost of ownership analyses, estimation frameworks, and pricing model inputs for managed security engagements across multiple cyber domains Designing differentiated deal structures and commercial models, including service tiers, outcome-based service level agreements, and cross-domain pricing strategies Collaborating with cyber subject matter specialists and pursuit stakeholders across Infrastructure Security, Identity and Access Management, Security Operations Center, Governance Risk and Compliance, and Vulnerability Management towers Supporting client discussions, orals, due diligence, and solution presentations that address threat landscape, regulatory obligations, security maturity gaps, and measurable risk reduction outcomes Working with transition and delivery teams to align pricing strategies with onboarding complexity, tool integration timelines, and steady-state managed security operations The team Cyber Operate Practice is focused on helping our clients identify and manage the cyber risks arising from their association with third parties or service providers. We help our clients to define their overall third-party cyber risk strategy, design and implement enterprise-wide programs and technology that focus on identifying and reducing risks; help them evaluate their objectives, priorities, strengths and weaknesses and roll out large scale organizational changes to achieve goals. Location: Bengaluru/Hyderabad/Pune/Chennai Qualifications Required: 9-15 years of experience in cybersecurity, including 6+ years in cybersecurity presales Experience solutioning enterprise cybersecurity services across multiple domains Experience developing business cases, estimation models, and pricing frameworks for managed security services Experience designing cyber managed service solutions across security domains Experience working in matrixed teams during deal pursuits with changing project requirements and timelines Advanced Microsoft Excel skills Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Cyber Security, Information Security, Engineering, Information Technology, or equivalent Preferred: Master of Business Administration (MBA)#Cyber_Cyber Operate

title: USI-EH27-GPS (G&P National)-L25-Services Associate- Market Business Relationship (EPM)- Hyderabad
description: Services Associate, Firm Risk, Compliance, and Governance Services- Market Business RelationshipDeloitte’s Enterprise Portfolio Management (EPM) team plays a crucial and growing role in helping the firm achieve its Multi-Disciplinary Model (MDM) objectives. EPM is seeking a high performing Analyst for a highly visible team working with senior business, industry, and account leaders to help modernize the Firm’s management of its third-party relationships to support critical MDM agility. The position sits in Growth & Purpose as part of the EPM team and is part of the larger Office of MDM that reports to the Chief Growth & Purpose Officer and business CEOs. The role offers a unique opportunity to help operationalize a new capability aimed at enabling Deloitte’s three world-class businesses to thrive. Work you'll do As a Services Associate, Firm Risk, Compliance, and Governance Services on the Enterprise Portfolio Management (EPM) team, you will be responsible for: Manage third-party risk data and processes, including data entry, verification, maintenance, audits, renewals, and closure of third-party engagement records in Deloitte compliance systems Support implementation of the MBR Modernization initiative to help operationalize a centralized capability that improves visibility into Deloitte’s sell-with relationships and supports growth Activate the new MBR operating model by gathering relationship information, confirming segmentation, identifying Relationship Owners, engaging LCSPs, and ensuring appropriate signaling in internal systems Engage with stakeholders across the firm to support the navigation of third-party relationships through the lifecycle, from early-stage discussions through establishment of strategic relationships Enable firm-wide visibility and accountability by managing high-volume MBR data and maintaining accuracy across firm systems throughout the relationship lifecycle The team EPM is a “heart of the business” multi-function, integrated team that brings deep MDM insights to tackle some of the firm’s most complex challenges. EPM’s mandate is to optimize our business footprint and unlock revenue by effectively navigating cross-business conflicts and Independence constraints while reducing MDM tension. Optimization requires EPM to proactively inform our business leaders on MDM implications for strategy and investment considerations, as well as vision to see beyond the firm’s current state to influence and optimize for our business of tomorrow. Location: Hyderabad Shift Timings: 2 PM to 11 PM Qualifications Required: 1+ years of professional experience in consulting, client service, project management, program management, compliance operations, or data management Experience maintaining records and data in business systems or compliance platforms Experience performing data entry, data verification, reporting, and record audits Experience coordinating with cross-functional stakeholders in a virtual team environment Strong English oral and written communication skills Ability to work the 2 PM to 11 PM shift Preferred: Experience supporting third-party risk, independence, compliance, governance, or marketplace business relationship processes Experience working with internal business systems and tools Experience supporting new process implementation or operating model changes Experience communicating with internal leadership, peers, and vendors Experience working in evolving or ambiguous business environments

title: USI-  EH27 - Consulting - Customer - S&S - PriceFX - Senior Consultant, Technical Transformation
description: #S&S2027Customer The Customer Team empowers organizations to build deeper relationships with customers through innovative strategies, advanced analytics, GenAI, transformative technologies, and creative design. We enable Deloitte client service teams to enhance customer experiences and drive sustained growth and customer value creation and capture, through customer and commercial strategies, digital product and innovation, marketing, commerce, sales, and service. We are a team of strategists, data scientists, operators, creatives, designers, engineers, and architects, balancing business strategy, technology, creativity, and ongoing managed services to help solve the biggest problems that impact customers, partners, constituents, and the workforce. We also offer Business Process as a Service, enabling organizations to streamline operations and achieve greater efficiency through scalable, technology-enabled managed insights that guide ongoing transformation and operational excellence. Position Summary We are seeking a Senior Consultant with experience in pricing transformation and Pricefx to support the design and delivery of pricing strategy, pricing technology, and commercial excellence solutions for clients. This role sits within the Sales & Service offering and supports Customer OP engagements focused on improving pricing effectiveness, margin performance, and sales execution. The Senior Consultant will work with cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into Pricefx-enabled solutions and support implementation, testing, and client adoption. Your role spans the full project lifecycle, including estimation, planning, execution, and tracking key metrics for analysis, ensuring high-quality and timely delivery of solutions. As a Senior Consultant, you will analyze complex business challenges, mentor team members, and uphold exceptional standards while building trusted client relationships and navigating increasingly complex situations. This role offers the opportunity to deepen your technical expertise and collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop and deliver impactful pricing strategies. Level: Senior Consultant, Technical Transformation Work you’ll do: Configure and extend Pricefx solutions to support enterprise pricing Collaborate with clients to analyze complex pricing challenges and deliver actionable recommendations. Support Pricefx implementations including requirements gathering, solution design, configuration, testing, and deployment Work with client stakeholders to assess current pricing processes, identify pain points, and define improvement opportunities Translate business requirements into functional specifications and user stories for pricing tools and workflows Assist in configuring Pricefx modules such as pricing logic, quoting, workflows, approvals, rebate management, analytics, and dashboards Conduct data analysis to support pricing strategy, segmentation, discount optimization, and margin improvement initiatives Collaborate with cross-functional teams on integrations across ERP, CRM, CPQ, and downstream reporting systems. Support SIT/UAT, and manage defect tracking and resolution Prepare client-ready deliverables including process maps, requirements documents, training materials, and status updates Support change management, end-user training, and business adoption activities Mentor junior team members and contribute to knowledge sharing and team capability building Collaborate with teams to drive impactful pricing strategies Help navigate complex project situations and drive execution of key project activities. Contribute to practice development through asset creation, internal knowledge sharing, and pursuit support The team: Step into our Sales & Service team, where technology powers business impact and innovation drives everything we do. As a global, dynamic group, we deliver seamless, end-to-end solutions—advising on strategy, implementing advanced CRM or back-office platforms, and enabling continuous business improvement. Here, you’ll collaborate with passionate, creative minds to transform sales models, elevate service excellence, and optimize customer operations. If you’re ready to grow your expertise, make bold ideas real, and help redefine the customer experience for leading organizations, this is your next big move. Join us and shape the future of sales and service. Qualifications Must Have Skills/Project Experience/Certifications: Bachelor’s degree in business, engineering, information systems, or related field 6+ years of consulting and/or industry experience in pricing, commercial excellence, revenue management, or sales transformation Hands-on experience with Pricefx in implementation, configuration, or business process design Understanding of pricing processes including price setting, discounting, approvals, rebates, and margin analysis Experience working with large datasets and translating insights into business recommendations Strong problem-solving, communication, and stakeholder management skills Ability to work in a team-based, client-facing environment and manage multiple workstreams Willingness to travel based on client and business needs Multiple end to end implementation experiences in Pricefx Should have very good communication and presentation skills  Good to Have Skills/Project Experience/Certifications: Experience with B2B pricing transformation programs Familiarity with ERP/CRM platforms such as SAP, Salesforce, Oracle, or Microsoft Dynamics Exposure to pricing analytics, CPQ, quote-to-cash, or commercial operations processes Experience in Agile delivery environments Advanced Excel, PowerPoint, and requirements documentation skills Prior experience in consulting, especially in customer, sales, or service transformation programs Pricefx certification preferred Understanding of Gitlab platform for project delivery Education: * BE/B.Tech/M.C.A./M.Sc (CS) degree or equivalent from accredited university Location: * Bengaluru/Hyderabad/Pune/Chennai

title: USI-EH27-R&BP-L25-Service Associate C&P Risk Management-Hyderabad
description: Work you'll do As a Services Associate, Firm Risk, Compliance, and Governance Services on the Risk and Brand Protection team, you will be responsible for: Supporting confidentiality and privacy activities, including review requests involving personal data removal and process guidance Coordinating appointments, resolving process issues, and overseeing successful transfer of personal data to personal devices Tracking support activities, reporting progress, and helping prepare periodic reporting and analytics Monitoring team mailbox activity, following up with stakeholders, and coordinating with U.S. and India teams as needed Assisting with process improvement activities, including updates to forms, templates, tools, and knowledge-sharing documents The team Deloitte Support Services India Private Ltd. (U.S India Enabling Areas (EA)) at the US India offices are the support arm of the organization and comprise several groups including Talent, Technology, Finance, Communications, Field Operations, etc. EA gives every client-service business unit access to the best and brightest resources when it comes to support services. Specifically, EA enables the business units to solely focus on satisfying clients and developing new products and services to sustain competitive advantage, while they consolidate and standardize a diverse collection of systems, processes, and functions. The team provides a wide array of services to the US firms and US India professionals, and is continually evaluating and expanding its portfolio. This group is responsible for supporting the Risk and Brand Protection driven operations which include Independence assurance and regulatory compliances of professionals of the US and India offices, internal audit function and developing and deploying programs to mitigate confidentiality and privacy risks. Location: Hyderabad Shift Timings: 2 PM to 11 PM Qualifications Required: Bachelor’s degree 1-3 years of experience in privacy, confidentiality, risk, compliance, or operational support activities Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint Experience supporting reporting, tracking, or operational documentation Experience coordinating with multiple internal stakeholders Experience managing shared mailboxes, inquiries, or case-related activities Preferred: B. Com MBA Knowledge of privacy and security principles and requirements Risk and compliance experience Familiarity with ServiceNow

title: USI-EH27-R&BP-L25 - Service Associate Canada Support Hub -Hyderabad
description: As a Services Associate, Firm Risk, Compliance, and Governance Services on the RBP team, you will be responsible for: Ensuring the quality and completeness of data related to clients and related parties in firm independence systems Supporting practitioners with internal tools by resolving issues ranging from simple updates to complex data and workflow challenges Managing tasks such as tagging, correcting account details, and reopening opportunities for new or existing accounts Following firm sales and opportunity governance requirements and completing assigned work accurately Saving required documentation and coordinating follow-ups with the appropriate teams as needed The teamThe RBP group plays a critical role in enhancing, preserving and protecting the reputation of the Deloitte organization. Our group is focused on strategic, confidential and regulatory risk matters which include ensuring Independence of the organization, Partners, Directors, Senior Managers and Managers, apart from conducting a host of compliance monitoring activities for all the professionals in the U.S, India and other member firm offices. The Centre of Excellence comprises various teams operating databases with client and affiliate information, monitoring conflicts of interest, monitoring data privacy, and monitoring investments of organization personnel. The group supports the Deloitte U.S. National Office as well as global Deloitte member firms.We are in an internal Deloitte U.S. Firms professional service position that supports member firms of the Deloitte organization. Functions within RBP include Independence Operations, Independence Audits, Conflicts, Independence Representations, Discipline, and Ethics & Compliance. The team interacts/coordinates frequently with other members across the member firms’ risk and regulatory affairs groups to effectively serve Deloitte professionals.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 2 PM to 11 PMQualificationsRequired: Master of Business Administration (MBA) or Post Graduate Diploma in Management (PGDM) in Finance with 2-3 years of experience 60% or 6.5 GPA and above throughout education without running backlogs Experience maintaining, validating, or updating client, account, or related-party data in business systems Experience using Microsoft Office applications, including Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint Experience completing documentation, follow-ups, and task tracking in a professional environment Ability to work 2 PM to 11 PM shift timings Preferred: Experience in independence, compliance, risk, or governance support activities Experience resolving data, workflow, or account maintenance issues in internal systems Experience supporting sales, opportunity governance, or account administration processes Experience working with database tools or analytics applications Experience supporting cross-functional teams in a deadline-driven environment

description: Join our Industry and Sectors team as a Campaign Management Specialist supporting the ER&I and FSI industries. In this role, you will lead and coordinate campaign activity, strengthen client relationships, activate differentiated brand positioning, and support high-quality execution across marketing initiatives. title: USI-EH-FY27- L45 - NSE TAX Pursuit - Senior, Tax Services
description: Pursuit - Senior, Tax ServicesAbout Deloitte GES and GPO Deloitte's Global Employment Solutions (GES) practice is one of the world’s largest, serving more than 300 multinational organizations. Our professionals work with companies operating internationally to design and administer human resources and tax programs and policies that support employees living and working outside their home countries. Within GES, the Global Payroll Operate (GPO) team provides both domestic and international payroll services, including payroll technology and process implementation, ongoing operations management, and coordination between internal and external teams.Role Overview This role is responsible for supporting and managing complex, strategic payroll pursuits across the full sales lifecycle. In addition to bid execution, the individual will help shape differentiated client propositions, drive pursuit governance, improve proposal quality, and support stronger win outcomes on strategic opportunities. The role requires close collaboration with account teams, solution leaders, commercial stakeholders, delivery SMEs, and leadership to ensure a compelling, compliant, and commercially sound pursuit process.Key Responsibilities1) Bid Management and Pursuit Coordination Respond to RFx opportunities, including proposal authoring and presentation support. Project-manage pursuits across the full sales lifecycle, acting as the central point of coordination for stakeholders to drive bid outcomes, manage reviews, and ensure effective communication from qualification through submission and deal closure. Develop and own the pursuit plan, including key milestones, deliverables, interdependencies, and accountability across contributors, and manage pursuit meetings and logistics. Establish and maintain pursuit working files/sites using tools such as Microsoft Teams, SharePoint, and other approved collaboration platforms. Contribute to win strategy development, value proposition refinement, solution reviews, oral presentations, negotiations, and business case preparation. Deliver pricing quote requests with a solid understanding of key commercial elements. Participate in both upstream and downstream bid and business development activities across all sales stages to ensure a consistent sales support experience. 2) Governance, Quality, and Compliance Own pursuit governance, including meetings, review checkpoints, leadership touchpoints, and overall bid logistics to drive timely decisions and progress. Provide end-to-end oversight and quality assurance for proposals and client-facing materials, ensuring alignment to strategy, compliance with RFx/RFP requirements, and adherence to risk and governance standards. Proactively track, escalate, and resolve risks, issues, and dependencies, coordinating with pursuit teams, leadership, and design/production partners to enable high-quality, on-time submissions. 3) Sales Enablement and Continuous Improvement Leverage industry insights, automation, tools, and best practices to improve the sales enablement experience. Apply leading practices and lessons learned from prior pursuits to strengthen bid quality, build team capability, and enhance pursuit and sales excellence across the practice. Experience Required Minimum 6–8 years of global payroll presales support experience. Experience in bid management and sales enablement support across multiple sales stages, preferably within an IT or payroll presales function. Strong stakeholder management skills, with the credibility and confidence to engage senior leaders, partners, client sponsors, procurement teams, and delivery stakeholders. Comprehensive understanding of payroll business processes, HRIS technologies, integration of HR systems with payroll systems, payroll tool implementation, transition management, and IT enablers and constraints, with the ability to collaborate and drive change within an international matrix environment. Strong proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint and other Microsoft tools to deliver high-quality sales materials to clients. Experience writing and editing content for strategic sales opportunities, proposals, and executive-level presentations. Proven ability to work creatively and analytically in a problem-solving environment, balancing client needs and operational feasibility. Strong collaborative style and the ability to influence across levels and functions. Key characteristics for success include proactiveness, enthusiasm, commitment, perseverance, adaptability, strong attention to detail, and negotiation skills. Master’s degree (or equivalent). Shift Timings: 11:00 AM to 8:00 PM IST Work Location: Hyderabad / Bangalore
qualifications: Pursuit - Senior, Tax ServicesAbout Deloitte GES and GPO Deloitte's Global Employment Solutions (GES) practice is one of the world’s largest, serving more than 300 multinational organizations. Our professionals work with companies operating internationally to design and administer human resources and tax programs and policies that support employees living and working outside their home countries. Within GES, the Global Payroll Operate (GPO) team provides both domestic and international payroll services, including payroll technology and process implementation, ongoing operations management, and coordination between internal and external teams.Role Overview This role is responsible for supporting and managing complex, strategic payroll pursuits across the full sales lifecycle. In addition to bid execution, the individual will help shape differentiated client propositions, drive pursuit governance, improve proposal quality, and support stronger win outcomes on strategic opportunities. The role requires close collaboration with account teams, solution leaders, commercial stakeholders, delivery SMEs, and leadership to ensure a compelling, compliant, and commercially sound pursuit process.Key Responsibilities1) Bid Management and Pursuit Coordination Respond to RFx opportunities, including proposal authoring and presentation support. Project-manage pursuits across the full sales lifecycle, acting as the central point of coordination for stakeholders to drive bid outcomes, manage reviews, and ensure effective communication from qualification through submission and deal closure. Develop and own the pursuit plan, including key milestones, deliverables, interdependencies, and accountability across contributors, and manage pursuit meetings and logistics. Establish and maintain pursuit working files/sites using tools such as Microsoft Teams, SharePoint, and other approved collaboration platforms. Contribute to win strategy development, value proposition refinement, solution reviews, oral presentations, negotiations, and business case preparation. Deliver pricing quote requests with a solid understanding of key commercial elements. Participate in both upstream and downstream bid and business development activities across all sales stages to ensure a consistent sales support experience. 2) Governance, Quality, and Compliance Own pursuit governance, including meetings, review checkpoints, leadership touchpoints, and overall bid logistics to drive timely decisions and progress. Provide end-to-end oversight and quality assurance for proposals and client-facing materials, ensuring alignment to strategy, compliance with RFx/RFP requirements, and adherence to risk and governance standards. Proactively track, escalate, and resolve risks, issues, and dependencies, coordinating with pursuit teams, leadership, and design/production partners to enable high-quality, on-time submissions. 3) Sales Enablement and Continuous Improvement Leverage industry insights, automation, tools, and best practices to improve the sales enablement experience. Apply leading practices and lessons learned from prior pursuits to strengthen bid quality, build team capability, and enhance pursuit and sales excellence across the practice. Experience Required Minimum 6–8 years of global payroll presales support experience. Experience in bid management and sales enablement support across multiple sales stages, preferably within an IT or payroll presales function. Strong stakeholder management skills, with the credibility and confidence to engage senior leaders, partners, client sponsors, procurement teams, and delivery stakeholders. Comprehensive understanding of payroll business processes, HRIS technologies, integration of HR systems with payroll systems, payroll tool implementation, transition management, and IT enablers and constraints, with the ability to collaborate and drive change within an international matrix environment. Strong proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint and other Microsoft tools to deliver high-quality sales materials to clients. Experience writing and editing content for strategic sales opportunities, proposals, and executive-level presentations. Proven ability to work creatively and analytically in a problem-solving environment, balancing client needs and operational feasibility. Strong collaborative style and the ability to influence across levels and functions. Key characteristics for success include proactiveness, enthusiasm, commitment, perseverance, adaptability, strong attention to detail, and negotiation skills. Master’s degree (or equivalent). Shift Timings: 11:00 AM to 8:00 PM IST Work Location: Hyderabad / Bangalore

title: USI-EH-27- UK TAX - L-55  Manager -ATT - TAX Services Hyderabad/Bangalore
description: UK TAX - Manager - Tax Services - Hyderabad/BangaloreDo you have a passion to work for U.K.-based clients of Deloitte Tax to provide tax compliance services? Are you ready to take the next step in your career by focusing on providing business and individual tax return compliance? Are you ready to fulfill your potential and want to have a significant impact to global initiatives? If the answer to all of the above is "Yes," come join us!Deloitte Tax Services India Private Limited commenced operations in June 2004. Since then, nearly all of the Deloitte Tax LLP ("Deloitte Tax") U.S. service lines and regions have obtained support through Deloitte Tax in India. Deloitte Tax in India offers you opportunities to learn and support practice U.S. taxation, a much sought-after career option. In recent years, member firms, such as Australia, Belgium, Canada, Germany, the Netherlands, the United Kingdom, etc. have obtained support in their tax related practice through the Deloitte Tax in India.At Deloitte, we are leading clients through the tax transformation taking place in the marketplace. We offer a broad range of fully integrated tax services and add greater impact to clients by combining technology and tax technical resources to uncover insights and smarter solutions for navigating an increasingly complex global environment.Work you’ll doIncreasingly complex tax decisions can have a significant effect—positive or negative—on the future of clients’ business. Our approach combines insight and innovation from multiple disciplines with business and industry knowledge to help clients excel globally.To list some of key responsibilities: -• Accurate preparation of low complexity UK Corporation Tax returns and computations across a range of industries using the standard procedures.• Identification and documentation of issues and preparation of client queries ✓ Working as part of a wider team to complete required volumes of work• Other UK Corporation Tax compliance-related processing activities as requiredThe TeamThe Deloitte UK team based in Hyderabad specializes in three core areas: UK corporation tax return preparation, electronic tagging and review of UK statutory financial statements, and governance and other support work for global tax outsourcing projects. On taking up a position with the UK team you will have the opportunity to focus on at least two of these core specialisms.Qualifications Required:• Full time master's in business administration (MBA) in Finance or equivalent from reputed University• ATT Certification is mandatory and least 3 - 5 years of experience in experience in UK Corporate Tax returns• Excellent written and verbal communication skills• Strong accounting knowledge• Knowledge of Microsoft Office products — In particular, Excel and WordWork Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 11 AM to 8 PM IST | 2 PM to 11 PM IST #UKTax
