As a Tax Consultant II, you will support German payroll, employee tax, and benefits processes, with a focus on accurate payroll delivery, compliance, and client service. This role is well suited for professionals with end-to-end German payroll experience who want to build deeper expertise in payroll operations and stakeholder support. Work you'll do As a Tax Consultant II on the Global Employer Services team, you will be responsible for supporting end-to-end German payroll activities and related stakeholder coordination. Handle end-to-end German payroll processing for multiple clients, including payroll data, overtime, bonuses, and deductions. Stay updated on German payroll legislation and support compliance with applicable payroll requirements. Interact with clients to gather payroll-related information, address queries, and provide live support through chat, phone, and email. Maintain employee records and payroll data, perform audits, and investigate payroll discrepancies and issues. Generate payroll reports, analyze payroll data for trends and discrepancies, and collaborate with internal teams to resolve payroll-related challenges. The team Deloitte's Global Employer Services (GES) practice based in London provides practical and creative solutions to clients. The GES practice is made up of several specialist groups, including Payroll Service delivery (PSD) team. This entails collating Payroll data from various predefined sources, preparing payroll instructions and providing these to the client’s payroll provide. On completion of the payroll processing, Deloitte will complete reconciliation checks and then produce management information reports to the clients. PSD services are delivered to agreed timetables under client Service Level Agreements (SLAs). The payroll team also supports new client implementations and the set-up of the payroll technology platform to facilitate the delivery of BAU services. title: USI-EH-27-Tax-Belgium-Stat Accounting-L35 - Tax Consultant II-Hyderabad
description: Deloitte Tax Services India Private Limited (“USI Tax”) provides professionals the opportunity to support U.S. and international tax practices across a broad global network. As part of the International Accounting & Corporate Income Tax Services (IACTS) team, you will help deliver statutory accounting compliance and corporate tax support across more than 30 countries. In this role, you will work on financial reporting, local GAAP conversions, and statutory filing activities while collaborating with cross-border teams and multinational clients. This is an opportunity to build experience in a complex, fast-changing regulatory environment while supporting high-quality, compliant reporting.Work you'll doAs a Tax Consultant II on the International Accounting & Corporate Income Tax Services (IACTS) team, you will be responsible for supporting pan-European statutory accounting compliance and reporting activities. Prepare conversions of accounts from management GAAP, including U.S. GAAP or International Financial Reporting Standards, to local GAAP, including preparation of statutory financial statements and support for timely filing. Assist clients with centralized and standardized statutory reporting packages using Deloitte tools, including BYG, Wdesk, and Intela. Coordinate with local GAAP specialists on country-specific accounting treatments to support compliant local financial statements. Work with multinational clients and cross-border teams to identify reporting challenges, assess tax considerations, and support statutory accounting requirements across jurisdictions. Analyze journals, trial balances, reconciliations, and other financial data to support reporting accuracy and broader financial statement review activities. The teamThe International Accounting & Corporate Income Tax Services (IACTS) team is a recognized Center of Excellence for pan-European statutory accounting compliance and corporate tax work. Based in Belgium, the team supports businesses with day-to-day financial reporting and compliance needs across multiple jurisdictions. Team members work in a multidisciplinary environment and help clients address complex accounting and tax challenges with practical, high-quality support.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 11 AM to 8 PMQualificationsRequired: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in economics, accountancy, finance, or another financial discipline Experience preparing or supporting statutory financial statements Experience converting accounts from management GAAP, including U.S. GAAP or International Financial Reporting Standards, to local GAAP Experience analyzing journals, trial balances, reconciliations, and other financial data Knowledge of U.S. GAAP, International Financial Reporting Standards, or a European GAAP English language proficiency Experience working with multinational stakeholders or cross-border teams Preferred: Experience using BYG, Wdesk, or Intela French language proficiency Dutch language proficiency Experience supporting statutory accounting or compliance activities for European entities

title: USI-EH-27-Tax-Belgium-Stat Accounting-L45 - Senior-Hyderabad
description: Deloitte Tax Services India Private Limited (“USI Tax”) offers professionals the opportunity to support U.S. and international tax practices across a broad global network. In this role, you will support statutory accounting and financial statement preparation activities for cross-border engagements, with exposure to multiple jurisdictions including Belgium and other European markets. You will work in a tax transformation environment that combines technology and tax technical resources to support reporting requirements, operational efficiency, and high-quality client service.Work you'll doAs a Senior, Tax Services on the statutory accounting team, you will be responsible for supporting statutory accounting compliance and financial statements preparation activities. You prepare the conversion of accounts from management GAAP (USGAAP or IFRS) to local/any European GAAP through the preparation of statutory financial statements. You share knowledge with junior team members and assist your managers with the delivery of accounting services to an international client portfolio. GAAP Conversion Preparation (Bridge files): Convert accounts from management GAAP (US GAAP or IFRS) to local or any European GAAP (e.g., Belgium, French, German, UK, etc.); Statutory Financial Statements: Prepare statutory financial statements and notes disclosures. Quality Risk and Compliance: Adhere to 100% of procedures for Quality Risk and compliance by following guidance from checklists; managing to plan and deliver work based on the individual calendars available; collaborating and frequent interaction with your colleagues across different locations for the delivery of statutory financial statements. Proficiency in reporting tools: Demonstrate hands-on experience with GAAP conversion and financial statements reporting tools such as Workiva, Thomson Reuters, CaseWare, and similar platforms; Data Management: Efficiently manage and process financial data using advanced Excel functions and other data analysis software; Automation and Efficiency: Leverage technology to automate routine tasks and enhance the efficiency of financial statements reporting processes. The teamThe statutory accounting practice at Hyderabad supports the Deloitte organization in Belgium who supports their client with statutory accounting compliance and financial statements preparation along with corporate income tax compliance processes. A comprehensive training will be provided to equip you with the necessary functional and technical skills.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 11 AM to 8 PMQualificationsRequired: Full time Bachelor’s (BCom) in Commerce or equivalent from reputed University with minimum of 60% and above or equivalent CGPA MBA Finance/ PGDBM Finance/MCOM – Full Time – 60% and Above CPT / CA Inter with 3 years Articleship experience Minimum of 4+ years of experience in a reputed firm. Excellent written and verbal communications skills at the business and technical level Knowledge of Microsoft Office products — Excel, Word, etc. Knowledge of financial reporting tools such as Workiva, Thomson Reuters, CaseWare, and similar platforms. Ability to multi-task various client responsibilities through prioritization of activities Capability of handling high work pressure during busy season Ability to integrate rapidly with existing team Solid analytical aptitude and problem-solving skills Good personal organizational skills and commitment to customer service Strong technical accounting knowledge Critical thinking and analytical skills Aptitude for learning technology

title: USI-EH27-R&BP-L25 - Service Associate Canada Audit -Hyderabad
description: As a Services Associate, Firm Risk, Compliance, and Governance Services on the Risk & Brand Protection team, you will be responsible for: Perform portions of personal independence audits for DTTL Canada Partners, Directors, and Managers by applying the audit process and reviewing submitted materials for compliance with DTTL Canada Independence policies. Identify financial interests in submitted audit materials, research entities using firm tools and internet sources, and document procedure results and potential policy exceptions. Work with independence team members to support best practices and build a consistent knowledge base for handling independence scenarios. Manage multiple auditees at the same time, track progress, and report status and issues to management. Develop working knowledge of DTTL Canada Independence policies to support conclusions on potential exceptions identified during the audit. The team The R&BP group plays a critical role in enhancing, preserving and protecting the reputation of the Deloitte organization. Our group is focused on strategic, confidential and regulatory risk matters which include ensuring Independence of the organization, Partners, Directors, Senior Managers and Managers, apart from conducting a host of compliance monitoring activities for all the professionals in the U.S, India and other member firm offices. The Centre of Excellence comprises various teams operating databases with client and affiliate information, monitoring conflicts of interest, monitoring data privacy, and monitoring investments of organization personnel. The group supports the Deloitte U.S. National Office as well as global Deloitte member firms. We are in an internal Deloitte U.S. Firms professional service position that supports member firms of the Deloitte organization. Functions within R&BP include Independence Operations, Independence Audits, Conflicts, Independence Representations, Discipline, and Ethics & Compliance amongst others. The team interacts/coordinates frequently with other members across the member firms’ risk and brand protection groups to effectively serve Deloitte professionals. Location: Hyderabad Shift Timings: 2 PM to 11 PM Qualifications Required: B.Com or CA Inter 2–3 years of experience in audit, compliance, independence, risk, or regulatory review Experience reviewing financial information and identifying policy exceptions Experience documenting audit procedures, findings, and case records Experience using Microsoft Office applications and database tools Experience handling confidential information in a professional environment Preferred: Experience supporting independence audits or compliance monitoring activities Experience researching financial interest entities using internal systems and internet sources Experience working with cross-functional or international stakeholders Experience managing multiple reviews, cases, or assignments simultaneously

title: USI-EH27-BO&I-L55-Specialist Executive Manager-Strategic Services
description: Business Operations & Integration (BO&I), US Operate – Specialist Executive ManagerDrive transformational change at one of the world's leading professional services firms. As an executive Strategic Services Manager, you'll lead high-impact strategic initiatives that span our cross-business operate and managed services (“Operate”) portfolios - advancing standardization, building operational consistency, and unlocking sustainable growth across the businesses. Work you’ll do  As an executive Strategic Services Manager, you will lead cross-business Risk Management initiatives that support business growth, enhance operational efficiency, and help scale our cross-business operate / managed services (“Operate”) offerings. You will serve as a strategic advisor partnering with cross-functional teams and senior leadership to shape the future of our Operate services. Your responsibilities will include:Developing and executing strategic plans to advance cross-business objectives and accelerate the growth of our Operate services offeringsPartner with senior leadership to align strategy with organizational goals and define high-impact strategic projects, initiatives, and investmentsManage and report on progress, outcomes, and business impact of strategic initiatives to executive stakeholdersDesign innovative solutions aligned with strategic objectives for cross-business Operate services - including vision setting, storyboarding, business case development, and point-of-view documentationIdentify and champion opportunities for innovation, standardization, and scalability across the Operate businessExtract and synthesize value-added insights from multiple data sources to inform strategic decision-makingLead cross-functional teams to implement strategic projects and drive adoption across business units and support functionsFacilitate identification and dissemination of leading practices from business-led Risk Management investmentsBuild strong collaborative relationships across the organization to ensure alignment and successful executionDesign and oversee a cross-business Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) dashboard across financial, operational, talent, technology, and service continuity domains to provide comprehensive visibility into cross-business Operate portfolio risksSupport implementing next-generation contract management requirements by leveraging Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) capabilities to proactively identify, assess, and mitigate cross-business portfolio risks across Operate servicesCollaborate with the firm-level ERM team to build synergies and ensure alignment of cross-business Operate portfolio risk management initiatives The team  You will be part of a dynamic, cross-business Operate team dedicated to driving impactful value and change throughout the organization. Our team values collaboration, creativity, and a result-oriented approach to solving complex business challenges.If you have experience in driving strategic initiatives and programs, and a passion for title: USI-EH27-Consulting-AI&E-HCI-EMS- Managed Services Engineer III-Apache Cassandra
description: Artificial Intelligence & EngineeringAI & Engineering leverages cutting-edge engineering capabilities to help build, deploy, and operate integrated/verticalized sector solutions in software, data, AI, network, and hybrid cloud infrastructure. These insights are powered by engineering for business advantage, helping transform mission-critical operations.    Join our AI & Engineering team to help transform technology platforms, driving innovation, and help make a significant impact on our clients' achievements. You’ll work alongside talented professionals reimagining and re-engineering operations and processes that could be critical to businesses. Position SummaryLevel: Senior Consultant As a Senior Consultant at Deloitte Consulting, you will design, develop, and deploy enterprise-scale software solutions, lead the creation of robust pipelines and manage code deployment across environments. You will collaborate with cross-functional, global teams to translate functional requirements into effective deliverables, independently guiding and mentoring junior team members. Your role spans the full project lifecycle, including estimation, planning, execution, and tracking key metrics for analysis, ensuring high-quality and timely delivery of solutionsWork you’ll do: A Senior DataStax Cassandra Database Administrator (DBA) is responsible for the design, deployment, management, and optimization of enterprise-wide Cassandra and DataStax Enterprise (DSE) clusters. This role demands extensive experience in NoSQL database technologies, performance tuning, and collaboration with development and operations teams.  The team: The DataStax administrator role involves setting up the cluster, optimization of the read and write data, copying data from other file systems Use technical knowledge to identify areas to improve. Provide flexible solutions to complex business problems using our technology practices. Collaborate closely with developers to implement requirements and provide necessary guidance. Database backup and restore. Handling missing critical DB scenario. At Hybrid Cloud Infrastructure we deliver solutions spanning Hybrid Cloud, Advanced Connectivity, AI Data Centers, High-Performance Computing, and AI Infrastructure to help clients achieve their desired outcomes. Our offerings include engineered transformation services for hybrid cloud infrastructure and platforms, prioritizing resiliency, optimization, and extensive automation. We integrate advanced connectivity with AI infrastructure and enterprise networks to boost operational efficiency and enable real-time data processing. Additionally, we provide comprehensive management of all facets of operations for hybrid cloud infrastructure and field operations.QualificationsMust Have Skills/Project Experience/Certifications: Minimum of 8 years of experience as a Database Administrator with expertise in Cassandra (Primary skill) , Redis and Kafka(secondary skill). Strong proficiency in database administration, including installation, configuration, and maintenance. In-depth knowledge of Cassandra architecture, data modeling, and query language (CQL). Experience with Redis data structures, replication, and clustering. Familiarity with database monitoring and management tools such as DataStax OpsCenter, Redis Enterprise Manager, and Confluent Control Center. Excellent troubleshooting and problem-solving skills. Strong communication and collaboration skills. Ability to work independently and prioritize tasks effectively in a dynamic environment. 8 - 10 years of hands-on experience in in Database Administration, with at least 8+ years dedicated to DataStax Casandra Database Administration. Good to Have Skills/Project Experience/Certifications: ·       Experience with any one of the following databases is required: Netezza, PostgreSQL, Informix, Mainframe, SQL Server, Oracle, Cosmos DB, IBM Db2 (UDB), or an Enterprise Data Warehouse.Education:BE / Btech /MCA M.Sc (CS) degree or equivalent from accredited university Location: Bengaluru / HyderabadShift Timings: ·       Rotational shifts 24*7#FY27ReferralCampaign#AIE2027

title: USI-EH27-GRC L40 Assistant Manager- Hyderabad
description: We are seeking a dedicated and experienced Assistant Manager to join our Cyber Governance, Risk, and Compliance team. The successful candidate will support cybersecurity risk oversight, control testing, and compliance activities across the organization, with a focus on incident response governance, risk management, security exceptions, and third-party risk management.Work you'll doAs a GRC Assistant Manager on the Cyber Governance, Risk, and Compliance team, you will be responsible for:Lead cybersecurity risk governance activities, including risk assessments across applications, platforms, and infrastructure, and maintain the cyber risk register.Design and execute security control testing aligned to established cybersecurity frameworks and organizational requirements.Track remediation activities, perform compliance reviews, and report control effectiveness and risk issues to stakeholders.Conduct vendor cyber risk assessments and support third-party risk monitoring activities across the supplier lifecycle.Support incident response governance and exception management activities, including post-incident reviews, risk evaluations, and compensating control reviews.The teamCyber Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) team. The successful candidate will play a pivotal role in managing cybersecurity risks, conducting control testing, and enhancing our overall cybersecurity posture, with a focus on incident response, risk management, security exception and third-party risk management.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 6:30 AM to 3:30 PMQualificationsRequired:Bachelor’s degree in Information Security, Cybersecurity, Computer Science, or a related field.6-8 years of experience in cybersecurity governance, risk management, compliance, or control testing.Experience performing cyber risk assessments across applications, infrastructure, or technology platforms.Experience executing security control testing or compliance assessment activities.Experience with cybersecurity frameworks such as International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 27001, National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) Cybersecurity Framework, or Center for Internet Security (CIS) Controls.Experience managing remediation tracking, risk registers, exception management, or third-party risk assessment activities.Preferred:Experience supporting incident response governance or post-incident review processes.Experience with third-party risk management programs and vendor security assessments.Experience with governance, risk, and compliance tools such as ServiceNow.Experience with cloud security, application security, or security architecture reviews.Experience applying Open Worldwide Application Security Project (OWASP) standards or DevSecOps practices.Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP), Certified Information Security Manager (CISM), or Certified in Risk and Information Systems Control (CRISC) certification.#CAL-KA

The successful candidate will play a pivotal role in managing cybersecurity risks, conducting control testing, and enhancing our overall cybersecurity posture, with a focus on incident response, risk management, security exception and third-party risk management.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 6:30 AM to 3:30 PMQualificationsRequired:Bachelor’s degree in Information Security, Cybersecurity, Computer Science, or a related field.6-8 years of experience in cybersecurity governance, risk management, compliance, or control testing.Experience performing cyber risk assessments across applications, infrastructure, or technology platforms.Experience executing security control testing or compliance assessment activities.Experience with cybersecurity frameworks such as International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 27001, National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) Cybersecurity Framework, or Center for Internet Security (CIS) Controls.Experience managing remediation tracking, risk registers, exception management, or third-party risk assessment activities.Preferred:Experience supporting incident response governance or post-incident review processes.Experience with third-party risk management programs and vendor security assessments.Experience with governance, risk, and compliance tools such as ServiceNow.Experience with cloud security, application security, or security architecture reviews.Experience applying Open Worldwide Application Security Project (OWASP) standards or DevSecOps practices.Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP), Certified Information Security Manager (CISM), or Certified in Risk and Information Systems Control (CRISC) certification.#CAL-KA

title: USI-EH27-Tax-Canada BTS-Corp Tax-L55 - Manager-Hyd/Blr/Ggn/Kol
description: Deloitte Tax in India supports U.S.-based Deloitte Tax clients across a broad range of tax services, offering professionals the opportunity to build deep experience in U.S. taxation. In this role, you will help clients navigate tax transformation, improve tax operations, and address an increasingly complex global environment. This is an opportunity to contribute to high-impact client work while growing your career within Deloitte’s Business Tax Services group in India.Work you'll doAs a Manager, Tax Services on the Business Tax Services team, you will be responsible for supporting U.S.-based Deloitte Tax clients across corporate, mutual fund, and hedge fund tax engagements. Provide tax compliance services, including income tax return preparation, computations, projections, and tax accounting support for U.S. federal, state, and international tax matters. Perform detail-level reviews of tax research, regulated investment company tax work papers, financial statement provisions, related tax disclosures, federal and relevant state returns, excise calculations, and regulated investment company compliance tests. Perform detail-level review of partnership work papers, allocations, and tax returns, and prepare materials and research for consulting projects related to tax planning opportunities. Coordinate and communicate with client contacts to gather information required to complete tax returns and support client service delivery. Supervise tax consultants, support training and mentoring of staff, and attend required Deloitte Tax LLP and sector-specific training. The teamAccelerating globalization, growing regulatory and business complexity and the evolution of tax technology is creating ever-increasing expectations for tax executives. At Deloitte, we are leading clients through change, uncovering insights and providing strategic value to organizations. Our approach combines insight and innovation from multiple disciplines with business and industry knowledge to help serve our clients with distinction. You will be based in India and are part of the US team serving our U.S. based clients.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: 7 AM to 4 PM | 11 AM to 8 PM | 2 PM to 11 PMQualificationsRequired:8+ years of experience in US Federal Tax - Corporate, Partnership, Private Wealth, Trust, Investment Management Hedge Funds, Private Equity, Mutual Funds, Regulated Investment Companies - Forms 1120, 1065Enrolled Agent or other appropriate certification (or in the process of pursuing)Bachelor’s degree in accounting, Finance, or another business-related fieldPrior Big 4 experience a plus or equivalent experienceKnowledge of fund accounting and general ledger accounting a plusStrong communication, interpersonal, and organizational skillsStrong analytical and communication skills to be able to articulate conclusions, planning opportunities, and risks identified through the research projects.Experience with managing projects including scheduling, budgeting, client correspondence and billing, excellent technology (i.e., Microsoft Excel and compliance software), research and writing skills.A preparer tax identification number (PTIN) is required for tax returns. If you do not have a PTIN or if your PTIN is expired, you are requested to complete the new or renewal application process (as applicable) before joining Deloitte.

title: USI-EH27-Tax-Canada BTS-Corp Tax-L35 - Tax Consultant II-Kolkata
description: Do you want to build a career serving U.S.-based clients while advancing your experience in U.S. taxation? As a Tax Consultant II within Deloitte Tax in India, you will support tax compliance, reporting, and advisory activities for clients across a range of industries and investment structures. This role offers the opportunity to work with cross-functional teams, develop technical tax knowledge, and contribute to client service in a dynamic, global environment. At Deloitte, we combine tax technical experience with technology-enabled solutions to help clients address complexity and drive business value.Work you'll doAs a Tax Consultant II on the Business Tax Services team, you will be responsible for… Preparing and reviewing U.S. federal, state, and international tax compliance workpapers, calculations, and returns for corporate and investment management clients. Supporting tax compliance engagements involving business tax returns, tax projections, and tax accounting activities. Performing detail-level review of tax research, regulated investment company compliance workpapers, excise tax calculations, and tax-related financial statement disclosures. Preparing partnership tax workpapers, allocations, and returns, and supporting research and analysis for tax planning and advisory projects. Coordinating with client contacts and internal teams to gather, validate, and organize information required to complete tax filings and related deliverables. The teamAccelerating globalization, growing regulatory and business complexity and the evolution of tax technology is creating ever-increasing expectations for tax executives. At Deloitte, we are leading clients through change, uncovering insights and providing strategic value to organizations. Our approach combines insight and innovation from multiple disciplines with business and industry knowledge to help serve our clients with distinction. You will be based in India and are part of the US team serving our U.S. based clients.Location: KolkataShift Timings: 7 AM to 4 PM | 11 AM to 8 PM | 2 PM to 11 PM Qualifications - Required: 8+ years of experience in US Federal Tax - Corporate, Partnership, Private Wealth, Trust, Investment Management - Hedge Funds, Private Equity, Mutual Funds, Regulated Investment Companies - Forms 1120, 1065 Enrolled Agent or other appropriate certification (or in the process of pursuing) Bachelor’s degree in accounting, Finance, or another business-related field Prior Big 4 experience a plus or equivalent experience Knowledge of fund accounting and general ledger accounting a plus Strong communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills Strong analytical and communication skills to be able to articulate conclusions, planning opportunities, and risks identified through the research projects. Experience with managing projects including scheduling, budgeting, client correspondence and billing, excellent technology (i.e., Microsoft Excel and compliance software), research and writing skills. title: USI - EH27 - Tax - GESTC - L25 - Quality Assurance Engineer I - Hyderabad
description: Quality Assurance Engineer l - Global Employer Services Technology Center (GESTC) Deloitte Tax Services India Private Limited (“Deloitte Tax in India”) commenced operations in June 2004. Since then, nearly all the Deloitte Tax LLP (“Deloitte Tax”) U.S. service lines and regions have obtained support services through Deloitte Tax in India. We provide support through the tax transformation taking place in the marketplace. We offer a broad range of fully integrated tax services by combining technology and tax technical resources to uncover insights and smarter solutions for navigating an increasingly complex global environment. We provide opportunities to transform tax operations using contemporary technologies in the market. Individuals work to transform their current state of tax to the next generation of tax functions. Are you ready to take the next step in your career to find new methods and processes to assist clients in improving their tax operations using new technologies? If the answer is “Yes,” come join Global Employer Services Technology Center (GESTC) Job purpose: This is an experience level position, and you will be responsible for manual and Automation testing to identify issues/bugs during pre and post application(s) release. You will be responsible for the quality engineering and testing of implementation that meets the needs and expectations of the business groups.  At the same time, you will be responsible to lead a testing team. Our applications are used by Deloitte’s Global Employer Services member firms and clients, so the successful candidate should have strong client service and communication skills, as well as the ability to deliver quality results on time in a dynamic and fast-paced team environment. The candidate will receive training on existing as well as next generation tools. Key job responsibilities  o   Become a subject matter expert in the domains in which you are working – this includes testing of various types, application functionality, and underlying business needso   Review requirements for completeness and accuracyo   Work with QA leads to understand and create the required test scenarios o   Design test cases to related to those test scenarios o   Execute test cases and record defectso   Be an active team member, driving quality, security, and best practices o   Responsible for various testing types which include but are not limited to :o   Functionalo   Smoke and Regressiono   Non-functional testing (e.g., usability, accessibility, performance)o   Helps troubleshoot defects and retest as fixes are provided o   Documents and evaluates test resultso   Utilize applications such as MS Excel to generate metrics and develop actionable recommendations  Education/Background:  Bachelor’s Degree educated or equivalent in Computer Science or related field     Key skills desired  Must Have:  o   Minimum 1-2 years of software testing experience o   Knowledge of the overall software development life cycle (Scaled Agile framework) o   Experience with testing methodologies o   Experience in test management tools, preferably Azure DevOps (ADO) o   Excellent written and oral communication skills  o   Comfortable working in a dynamic team environment where projects, and deadlines often require multi-tasking and flexible work schedules o   Ability to integrate rapidly with existing team    Good to have:  o   Quality Assurance certifications such as ISTQB are a plus o   Automation scripting using TOSCA or other low-code automation toolso   Meticulous attention to detail and quality o   An ability to build professional relationships, a spirit of co-operation, and a flexible approach to work are required  o Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical thinking skills; outstanding analytical, research and presentation skills #TechnologyFY26

title: USI-EH27-UKHRS-HR Admin-Associate Analyst
description: Job Title: Associate Analyst/Analyst - Deloitte Support Services India Private Limited (Irish MF) Location: Hyderabad Job Type: Full-Time (1:30 PM to 10:30 PM IST / 2:00 PM to 11:00 PM IST) Introduction: We are seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented HR Support Specialist to join our Human Resources Support Services (HRSS) team. The ideal candidate will be committed to delivering exceptional customer service by managing and executing a variety of HR-related tasks. This role demands a focus on delivering high-quality services within agreed timelines and maintaining strict adherence to GDPR guidelines. Experience with Workday is highly preferred, as it will be integral to daily operations, ensuring efficient and timely processing that meets our clients' expectations. Key Responsibilities (such as but not limited to): • Reference Letters: Prepare and issue employment reference letters, ensuring accuracy and timely delivery to both current and former employees. • Salary Certificates: Generate and distribute salary certificates as requested by employees, ensuring all information is accurate and provided within a strict timeframe. • Coach Changes: Administer changes in coaching assignments within the organization, coordinating effectively with relevant departments / stakeholders to ensure smooth transitions. • Bike to Work Scheme: Manage the organization’s Bike to Work scheme, facilitating employees and handling related administrative tasks / requests. • Eyecare/Specsavers VDU Vouchers: Oversee the management of eyecare vouchers, ensuring compliance with organizational policies and health regulations. • Personal Milestones – Perx Vouchers, Flowers, and Baby Gifts: Coordinate the acknowledgment and celebration of personal milestones for employees, including the distribution of Perx vouchers, flowers, and baby gifts, ensuring personalization and timely recognition etc. You may be assigned additional or ad-hoc responsibilities related to the Ireland/USI processes as may be transitioned to you or deemed necessary by the organization (Ireland/USI). Where such tasks require the application of your training, expertise, or process knowledge, you shall be responsible for executing, managing, and completing these requests in accordance with established standards and operational requirements. Skills and Qualifications: • Bachelor’s degree in human resources, Business Administration, or a related field. • Proven experience in Human Resources, particularly in an HR support role. • Strong working knowledge of HR systems, preferably Workday. • Excellent organizational and time managementskills, with the ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously and meet tight deadlines. • Strong attention to detail and commitment to accuracy. • Familiarity with General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and other relevant privacy laws, demonstrating the ability to handle sensitive information with discretion and integrity. • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, capable of working effectively with diverse teams. Preferred Qualifications: • Previous experience in handling HR tasks related to benefits, employee engagement, or compliance. • Experience in a multinational corporation (preferred). How you will grow At Deloitte, we have invested a great deal to create a rich environment in which our professionals can grow. We want all our people to develop in their own way, playing to their own strengths as they hone their leadership skills. And, as a part of our efforts, we provide our professionals with a variety of learning and networking opportunities—including exposure to leaders, sponsors, coaches, and challenging assignments—to help accelerate their careers along the way. No two people learn in the same way. So, we provide a range of resources including live classrooms, team- based learning, and eLearning. DU: The Leadership Center in India, our state-of-the-art, world-class learning Center in the Hyderabad offices is an extension of the Deloitte University (DU) in Westlake, Texas, and represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to our people’s growth and development. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India Benefits At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. We value our people and offer employees a broad range of benefits. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you. Deloitte’s culture Our positive and supportive culture encourages our people to do their best work every day. We celebrate individuals by recognizing their uniqueness and offering them the flexibility to make daily choices that can help them to be healthy, centered, confident, and aware. We offer well-being programs and are continuously looking for new ways to maintain a culture that isinclusive, invites authenticity, leverages our diversity, and where our people excel and lead healthy, happy lives. Learn more about Life at Deloitte. Corporate citizenship Deloitte is led by a purpose: to make an impact that matters. This purpose defines who we are and extends to relationships with our clients, our people and our communities. We believe that business has the power to inspire and transform. We focus on education, giving, skill-based volunteerism, and leadership to help drive positive social impact in our communities. Learn more about Deloitte’s impact on the world. Deloitte is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. This job description outlines the core responsibilities and requirements for the HR Support Specialist role, ensuring candidates are aware of the expectations and the importance of compliance and efficiency in their daily tasks.#mfnsehrsref
qualifications: Job Title: Associate Analyst/Analyst - Deloitte Support Services India Private Limited (Irish MF) Location: Hyderabad Job Type: Full-Time (1:30 PM to 10:30 PM IST / 2:00 PM to 11:00 PM IST) Introduction: We are seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented HR Support Specialist to join our Human Resources Support Services (HRSS) team. The ideal candidate will be committed to delivering exceptional customer service by managing and executing a variety of HR-related tasks. This role demands a focus on delivering high-quality services within agreed timelines and maintaining strict adherence to GDPR guidelines. Experience with Workday is highly preferred, as it will be integral to daily operations, ensuring efficient and timely processing that meets our clients' expectations. Key Responsibilities (such as but not limited to): • Reference Letters: Prepare and issue employment reference letters, ensuring accuracy and timely delivery to both current and former employees. • Salary Certificates: Generate and distribute salary certificates as requested by employees, ensuring all information is accurate and provided within a strict timeframe. • Coach Changes: Administer changes in coaching assignments within the organization, coordinating effectively with relevant departments / stakeholders to ensure smooth transitions. • Bike to Work Scheme: Manage the organization’s Bike to Work scheme, facilitating employees and handling related administrative tasks / requests. • Eyecare/Specsavers VDU Vouchers: Oversee the management of eyecare vouchers, ensuring compliance with organizational policies and health regulations. • Personal Milestones – Perx Vouchers, Flowers, and Baby Gifts: Coordinate the acknowledgment and celebration of personal milestones for employees, including the distribution of Perx vouchers, flowers, and baby gifts, ensuring personalization and timely recognition etc. You may be assigned additional or ad-hoc responsibilities related to the Ireland/USI processes as may be transitioned to you or deemed necessary by the organization (Ireland/USI). Where such tasks require the application of your training, expertise, or process knowledge, you shall be responsible for executing, managing, and completing these requests in accordance with established standards and operational requirements. Skills and Qualifications: • Bachelor’s degree in human resources, Business Administration, or a related field. • Proven experience in Human Resources, particularly in an HR support role. • Strong working knowledge of HR systems, preferably Workday. • Excellent organizational and time managementskills, with the ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously and meet tight deadlines. • Strong attention to detail and commitment to accuracy. • Familiarity with General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and other relevant privacy laws, demonstrating the ability to handle sensitive information with discretion and integrity. • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, capable of working effectively with diverse teams. Preferred Qualifications: • Previous experience in handling HR tasks related to benefits, employee engagement, or compliance. • Experience in a multinational corporation (preferred). How you will grow At Deloitte, we have invested a great deal to create a rich environment in which our professionals can grow. We want all our people to develop in their own way, playing to their own strengths as they hone their leadership skills. And, as a part of our efforts, we provide our professionals with a variety of learning and networking opportunities—including exposure to leaders, sponsors, coaches, and challenging assignments—to help accelerate their careers along the way. No two people learn in the same way. So, we provide a range of resources including live classrooms, team- based learning, and eLearning. DU: The Leadership Center in India, our state-of-the-art, world-class learning Center in the Hyderabad offices is an extension of the Deloitte University (DU) in Westlake, Texas, and represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to our people’s growth and development. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India Benefits At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. We value our people and offer employees a broad range of benefits. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you. Deloitte’s culture Our positive and supportive culture encourages our people to do their best work every day. We celebrate individuals by recognizing their uniqueness and offering them the flexibility to make daily choices that can help them to be healthy, centered, confident, and aware. We offer well-being programs and are continuously looking for new ways to maintain a culture that isinclusive, invites authenticity, leverages our diversity, and where our people excel and lead healthy, happy lives. Learn more about Life at Deloitte. Corporate citizenship Deloitte is led by a purpose: to make an impact that matters. This purpose defines who we are and extends to relationships with our clients, our people and our communities. We believe that business has the power to inspire and transform. We focus on education, giving, skill-based volunteerism, and leadership to help drive positive social impact in our communities. Learn more about Deloitte’s impact on the world. Deloitte is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. This job description outlines the core responsibilities and requirements for the HR Support Specialist role, ensuring candidates are aware of the expectations and the importance of compliance and efficiency in their daily tasks.#mfnsehrsref

title: USI-EH27- L35 -Tax Consultant II- UiPath, Power Automate, Python- Indirect Tax - Digital Enablement Team (DET)- Chennai
description: Do you have a passion to work for US based clients of Deloitte Tax and transform their current state of tax to the next generation of tax functions? Are you ready to take the next step in your career to find new methods and processes to assist clients in improving their tax operations? Are you ready to fulfill your potential and want to have a significant impact to global initiatives? If the answer to all of the above is “Yes,” come join Indirect Tax group in Deloitte India (Offices of the U.S), a service line of Deloitte Tax LLP!Deloitte Tax Services India Private Limited commenced operations in June 2004. Since then, nearly all of the Deloitte Tax LLP (“Deloitte Tax”) U.S. service lines have obtained support through Deloitte Tax in India. Deloitte Tax in India offers you opportunities to learn U.S. taxation, a much sought-after career option.At Deloitte, we are leading clients through the tax transformation taking place in the marketplace. We offer a broad range of fully integrated tax services and add greater impact to clients by combining technology and tax technical resources to uncover insights and smarter solutions for navigating an increasingly complex global environment.Work you’ll doTax Technology professionals at Deloitte combine insight and innovation from multiple disciplines with business and industry knowledge to help our clients excel globally. We are looking for driven individuals having a passion for technology - whether its programming applications, automation or datacenter operational excellence. If you like to figure out how to make the new technologies work in a corporate environment, then our team might be a fit for you.Key responsibilities will be: -Requirements Gathering: Collect and document tax requirements and assess automation feasibility.Stakeholder Engagement: Work directly with stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into technical specifications.Solution Design, Development: Design, develop, and deploy automation solutions utilizing UiPath Platform (Primarily), Microsoft Power Platform (Power Automate Cloud and Desktop) and Python.Custom Development: Create custom activities using VB .Net/C#.System Integration: Ensure seamless integration of RPA solutions with existing systems to facilitate consistent data flow.Process Optimization: Continuously evaluate and improve existing automation processes for enhanced efficiency and alignment with changing business needs.Project Delivery: Innovatively and collaboratively manage projects, escalate issues appropriately, and achieve business objectives efficiently.Training and Support: Provide necessary training and support to end-users and team members to ensure smooth adoption of automated processes.Automation Maintenance: Maintain an automated delivery/deployment model using a DevOps pipeline to enable continuous updates and release cycles.The TeamThe DET group is focused on delivering differentiated service offerings through the use of new technologies such as Data Wrangling, Data Analytics, Business Intelligence (BI), Robotic Process Automation (RPA), Optical Character Recognition (OCR), and Machine Learning. This group enables the Indirect Tax practice to drive profitability and growth, through application of emerging and existing technologies and enhanced utilization of Tax tools and software. We support Indirect tax teams by assessing and addressing their data and technology challenges on client engagements, internal processes and data intensive projects to help achieve efficiencies, win clients and create differentiated services. With the exponential growth of accessible data, there is an increasing need to bring data and technology capabilities to help address client needs and support Deloitte indirect Tax engagements and solutions.Qualification and Experience Required –ü  Full time Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) or equivalent degree in Computer Scienceü  UiPath Certified Advanced RPA Developer (UiARD) [not the UiPath academy diploma], UiPath Agentic Automationü  Power Automate RPA Developer Associate [PL-500]ü  Solid coding skills with Python [Browser Automation Using Selenium, browser API automation]ü  Minimum 3 years of experience in UiPath (with good knowledge on Agentic Automation), Power Automate, Python, SQLü  Knowledge of Git, Postmanü  Understanding of API featuresü  Strong knowledge on Relational and document-oriented databases (SQL Server, Oracle, Mongo DB etc.)ü  Strong analytical and problem-solving skillsü  Aggressively learn new technologies through self-study and work to develop prototypes and solve technical problemsü  Ability to perform data intensive tasks and be skilled to organize the data for reporting requirements – Tax Data Managementü  Effective communication with strong relationship management skillsü  Team player, adhering to the timelines for finishing deliverablesü  Relentless focus on quality of work products while adhering to completing deliverables on timePreferred:ü  Strong experience in building workflows to perform UI & API level automationü  Strong experience in data extraction from various ERP systems using UiPath/Power Automate.ü  Experience with accessing data from a variety of data sources, such as RDBMS/SQL, NOSQL, OLAP, flat files and logsü  Proficiency in writing SQL queries and Python scriptsü  Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence including UiPath Agentic Automation, experience to drive business insights and support data-driven decisions are an added advantageü  Experience on Cloud Technologies ü  Experience with Azure SQL Database [Table Storage] Work Location – Chennai Shift time:   11 am – 8 pmDo you have a passion to work for US based clients of Deloitte Tax and transform their current state of tax to the next generation of tax functions? Are you ready to take the next step in your career to find new methods and processes to assist clients in improving their tax operations? Are you ready to fulfill your potential and want to have a significant impact to global initiatives? If the answer to all of the above is “Yes,” come join Indirect Tax group in Deloitte India (Offices of the U.S), a service line of Deloitte Tax LLP!Deloitte Tax Services India Private Limited commenced operations in June 2004. Since then, nearly all of the Deloitte Tax LLP (“Deloitte Tax”) U.S. service lines have obtained support through Deloitte Tax in India. Deloitte Tax in India offers you opportunities to learn U.S. taxation, a much sought-after career option.At Deloitte, we are leading clients through the tax transformation taking place in the marketplace. We offer a broad range of fully integrated tax services and add greater impact to clients by combining technology and tax technical resources to uncover insights and smarter solutions for navigating an increasingly complex global environment.Work you’ll doTax Technology professionals at Deloitte combine insight and innovation from multiple disciplines with business and industry knowledge to help our clients excel globally. We are looking for driven individuals having a passion for technology - whether its programming applications, automation or datacenter operational excellence. If you like to figure out how to make the new technologies work in a corporate environment, then our team might be a fit for you.Key responsibilities will be: - Requirements Gathering: Collect and document tax requirements and assess automation feasibility. Stakeholder Engagement: Work directly with stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into technical specifications. Solution Design, Development: Design, develop, and deploy automation solutions utilizing UiPath Platform (Primarily), Microsoft Power Platform (Power Automate Cloud and Desktop) and Python. Custom Development: Create custom activities using VB .Net/C#. System Integration: Ensure seamless integration of RPA solutions with existing systems to facilitate consistent data flow. Process Optimization: Continuously evaluate and improve existing automation processes for enhanced efficiency and alignment with changing business needs. Project Delivery: Innovatively and collaboratively manage projects, escalate issues appropriately, and achieve business objectives efficiently. Training and Support: Provide necessary training and support to end-users and team members to ensure smooth adoption of automated processes. Automation Maintenance: Maintain an automated delivery/deployment model using a DevOps pipeline to enable continuous updates and release cycles. The Team      The DET group is focused on delivering differentiated service offerings through the use of new technologies such as Data Wrangling, Data Analytics, Business Intelligence (BI), Robotic Process Automation (RPA), Optical Character Recognition (OCR), and Machine Learning. This group enables the Indirect Tax practice to drive profitability and growth, through application of emerging and existing technologies and enhanced utilization of Tax tools and software. We support Indirect tax teams by assessing and addressing their data and technology challenges on client engagements, internal processes and data intensive projects to help achieve efficiencies, win clients and create differentiated services. title: USI FY27 | Cyber | Digital Trust & Privacy | Data Encryption-Hashicorp - L35
description: Deloitte Cyber helps organizations navigate the evolving threat landscape with greater confidence and resilience. As an Encryption-Hashicorp Security Engineer, you will support clients in strengthening secrets management, enterprise encryption, and public key infrastructure capabilities across complex environments. This role combines technical delivery, implementation, and client advisory support, with a strong emphasis on HashiCorp Vault. You will work with teams and clients to help protect sensitive data and advance cyber transformation priorities.Work you'll doAs an Encryption-Hashicorp Security Engineer on the Data and Digital Trust team, you will be responsible for… Implementing and enhancing HashiCorp Vault, encryption, and public key infrastructure solutions for client environments Supporting configuration, upgrades, administration, and integration of Vault and other encryption platforms and security tools Assisting clients with secrets management, data protection requirements, and encryption solution design Troubleshooting technical issues and supporting project delivery across implementation and operational activities Collaborating with client stakeholders and internal teams to drive execution, documentation, and solution adoption The teamData and Digital Trust practice is primarily focused on helping our clients to do effective utilization of data while safeguarding privacy and security. D&DT solutions prioritize transparency, privacy, and the ethical use of data, assisting organizations in driving sustainable digital transformation. Our solutions assist organizations in meeting the shifting expectations of consumers and create unprecedented business opportunities. We help our clients to define their overall Data protection and Privacy strategy, design global, pan-enterprise programs that focus on mitigating threats, evaluate their objectives, priorities, strengths and weaknesses and roll out large scale organizational changes to achieve goals. Our services capture work across advice, implement, and operate solutions. The Data and Digital Trust team delivers services to clients through following key areas:Privacy Governance & ComplianceData Protection & ComplianceDigital Trust & Online ProtectionRisk & Controls EnablementTechnology & ImplementationOur Data and Digital Trust solutions are designed to allow organizations to address requirements stemming from the ever-changing regulatory landscape, consumer expectations, and technological transformations and advancements.Location: Bengaluru / Hyderabad / Pune / ChennaiShift Timings: 11 AM to 8 PM, 2 PM to 11 PM, 5 PM to 2 AM ISTQualificationsRequired: 3-6 years of experience in encryption, secrets management, or public key infrastructure implementation Experience implementing and supporting HashiCorp Vault for secrets management, encryption, key management, or public key infrastructure use cases Experience with encryption or public key infrastructure tools such as DigiCert Trust Lifecycle Manager, Vormetric, Venafi, Keyfactor, AppViewX, Azure Key Vault, SandboxAQ, or hardware security modules Experience configuring, upgrading, and supporting encryption or public key infrastructure technologies Experience with Unix or Linux environments Experience with scripting or automation for platform administration, integration, or support Bachelor of Engineering, Bachelor of Technology, Master of Computer Applications, Master of Science in Computer Science, or equivalent degree#Cyber_Digital Trust & Privacy

title: USI - EH27 - Talent Services - Learning Operations - Services Specialist - Talent Processes and Business Support - Hyderabad
description: Are you passionate about transforming data into actionable insights and driving impactful business decisions? Do you thrive in a fast-paced environment, managing multiple projects with agility and accountability? If so, join our Learning Insights Team and help shape the future of Learning & Development (L&D) at Deloitte. At Deloitte, you’ll gain exposure to a variety of industries and business models, supporting your career growth and professional development. Work you'll do As a Services Specialist on the Learning Insights team, you will be responsible for: Develop and maintain data processes, models, and reporting assets to support Learning & Development initiatives. Build and enhance business intelligence solutions using Power BI, Tableau, Python, Microsoft Excel, and Power Query. Create and manage data pipelines and extract, transform, load (ETL) processes to improve data integrity, availability, and reporting accuracy. Translate business requirements into technical specifications for reports and dashboards, and perform data analysis to assess structure, content, and quality. Deliver routine and ad hoc reporting, support end-user adoption, and partner with business teams and system analysts to refine requirements. Apply project management practices to deliver solutions, address process concerns and escalations, and support data accuracy, security, and privacy requirements. The team Our team culture is collaborative and encourages members to take initiative and seek on-the-job learning opportunities. Our Learning Operations professionals are committed to delivering exceptional Quality of Service and ensuring client satisfaction by resolving requests promptly. Location: Hyderabad Qualifications Required: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Science, Business Analytics, or Learning Science. 3-4 years of experience in business intelligence, reporting, or analytics roles. Experience using Power BI, Tableau, Python, Microsoft Excel, Power Query, and Structured Query Language (SQL). Experience building dashboards, reports, data models, and extract, transform, load (ETL) processes. Experience analyzing data quality, data structures, and source-to-report mappings. Experience translating business requirements into technical specifications for reporting solutions. Experience supporting Learning & Development (L&D), Talent Development, Human Resources, or workforce operations. Preferred: Experience supporting Learning & Development (L&D) or Talent Development functions. Experience with machine learning models or predictive analytics. Experience with Learning Management Systems (LMS). Experience applying user interface/user experience (UI/UX) principles to dashboards or reporting solutions. Experience with strategic planning or resource planning in an operations environment. Experience using Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools for content creation, automation, or analytics workflows.

If so, join our Learning Insights Team and help shape the future of Learning & Development (L&D) at Deloitte. At Deloitte, you’ll gain exposure to a variety of industries and business models, supporting your career growth and professional development. Work you'll do As a Services Specialist on the Learning Insights team, you will be responsible for: Develop and maintain data processes, models, and reporting assets to support Learning & Development initiatives. Build and enhance business intelligence solutions using Power BI, Tableau, Python, Microsoft Excel, and Power Query. Create and manage data pipelines and extract, transform, load (ETL) processes to improve data integrity, availability, and reporting accuracy. Translate business requirements into technical specifications for reports and dashboards, and perform data analysis to assess structure, content, and quality. Deliver routine and ad hoc reporting, support end-user adoption, and partner with business teams and system analysts to refine requirements. Apply project management practices to deliver solutions, address process concerns and escalations, and support data accuracy, security, and privacy requirements. The team Our team culture is collaborative and encourages members to take initiative and seek on-the-job learning opportunities. Our Learning Operations professionals are committed to delivering exceptional Quality of Service and ensuring client satisfaction by resolving requests promptly. Location: Hyderabad Qualifications Required: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Science, Business Analytics, or Learning Science. 3-4 years of experience in business intelligence, reporting, or analytics roles. Experience using Power BI, Tableau, Python, Microsoft Excel, Power Query, and Structured Query Language (SQL). Experience building dashboards, reports, data models, and extract, transform, load (ETL) processes. Experience analyzing data quality, data structures, and source-to-report mappings. Experience translating business requirements into technical specifications for reporting solutions. Experience supporting Learning & Development (L&D), Talent Development, Human Resources, or workforce operations. Preferred: Experience supporting Learning & Development (L&D) or Talent Development functions. Experience with machine learning models or predictive analytics. Experience with Learning Management Systems (LMS). Experience applying user interface/user experience (UI/UX) principles to dashboards or reporting solutions. Experience with strategic planning or resource planning in an operations environment. Experience using Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools for content creation, automation, or analytics workflows.
hiring_organization: Deloitte | India Offices of the US

title: USI - EH27 - Cyber Security- GRC- Risk & Compliance- Assistant Manager - Hyderabad
description: Do you have the drive to engage in cyber security risk & compliance to protect organizations from potential security exposures or threats? If your answer is yes, we have the right opportunity for you!Work you'll doAs an Assistant Manager, GRC- Cyber Program Design and Governance on the Cyber Security team, you will be responsible for: Executing vendor information security risk assessments aligned to Deloitte’s governance, risk, and compliance objectives Reviewing vendor security programs, processes, and controls to identify risks to Deloitte data, systems, networks, and facilities Documenting assessment results, communicating findings to stakeholders, and tracking remediation plans through closure Collaborating with vendors, internal business stakeholders, subject matter specialists, and Cyber Security leadership across onshore and offshore teams Supporting vendor policy compliance activities and cyber security awareness initiatives across the organization The teamDeloitte Technology team helps power Deloitte’s success. Deloitte Technology team is the engine that helps to drive Deloitte, which serves many of the world’s largest organizations. We develop and deploy cutting-edge internal and go-to-market solutions that help Deloitte operate effectively and lead in the market. Our reputation is built on a tradition of delivering with excellence.The ~3,000 professionals in Deloitte Technology deliver services internally including: Cyber security Technology support Technology & Infrastructure Application development and management Relationship management group Strategy Deployment PMO Financials Cyber SecurityCyber Security vigilantly protects Deloitte and client data. The team leads a strategic cyber risk program that adapts to a rapidly changing threat landscape, changes in business strategies, risks, and vulnerabilities. Using situational awareness, threat intelligence, and building a security culture across the organization, the team helps to protect the Deloitte brand.Areas of Risk & Compliance focus include: Client ODCs Security Contract Reviews Vendor Assessments Security Awareness Audits & Assessments Location: HyderabadShift Timings: Indian Standard TimeQualificationsRequired: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Business Administration, or equivalent educational or professional experience 6-9 years of experience in information security, technology risk, governance, risk, and compliance, vendor risk management, audit, controls testing, risk assessment, or remediation management Experience reviewing third-party or vendor information security programs, processes, and controls Experience with control frameworks and regulatory standards, including Service Organization Control 2 (SOC 2), International Organization for Standardization 27001 (ISO 27001), National Institute of Standards and Technology 800-53 (NIST 800-53), Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (PCAOB), or Cloud Security Alliance (CSA) Experience with Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) tools Experience preparing assessment reports, findings, and remediation tracking for stakeholders Experience assessing security controls for cloud services Preferred: Experience implementing information security programs Experience conducting information security audits or assessments Experience supporting vendor remediation management activities Industry certifications such as Certified Information Systems Auditor (CISA), Certified Information Security Manager (CISM), Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP), Certified Cloud Security Professional (CCSP), Certified Ethical Hacker (CEH), ISO 27001, ISO 27002, or ISO 22301

title: USI - EH27 - Cyber Security- Cloud Security - Assistant Manager - Hyderabad
description: Are you passionate about technology and interested in joining a community of collaborative colleagues who respectfully and courageously seek to challenge the status quo? If so, read on to learn more about an exciting opportunity with Deloitte Technology US (DT - US). We are curious and life-long learners focused on technology and innovation.Work you'll doAs an Assistant Manager, Cyber Engineering on the Cyber Security team, you will be responsible for: Collaborating with application, Cyber Security, and infrastructure teams to onboard applications into the microsegmentation service Monitoring the microsegmentation mailbox and ServiceNow queue and responding to requests and incidents Diagnosing and resolving microsegmentation issues and supporting timely operational needs Evaluating and implementing changes to microsegmentation security policies based on risk and business requirements Researching application traffic to identify communication flows and support policy design Drafting and maintaining process and procedure documentation and recommending improvements to microsegmentation operations The teamDeloitte Technology US (DT - US) helps power Deloitte’s success, which serves many of the world’s largest, most respected organizations. We develop and deploy cutting-edge internal and go-to-market solutions that help Deloitte operate effectively and lead in the market. Our reputation is built on a tradition of delivering with excellence.The ~3,000 professionals in DT - US deliver services including Cyber Security, Technology Support, Technology & Infrastructure, Applications, Relationship Management, Strategy & Communications, Project Management, and Financials.Cyber Security vigilantly protects Deloitte and client data. The team leads a strategic cyber risk program that adapts to a rapidly changing threat landscape, changes in business strategies, risks, and vulnerabilities. Using situational awareness, threat intelligence, and building a security culture across the organization, the team helps to protect the Deloitte brand.Areas of focus include Risk & Compliance, Identity & Access Management, Data Protection, Cyber Design, Incident Response, Security Architecture, and Business Partnership.Location: HyderabadShift Timings: Indian Standard working hoursQualificationsRequired: Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Engineering, or a specified technical field 6-8 years of experience in Cyber Security or Network Operations Experience with networking concepts, including protocols and firewall operations Experience supporting application or infrastructure security operations Experience troubleshooting technical issues in enterprise environments Preferred: Experience supporting or operating microsegmentation or network security solutions Experience with ServiceNow or similar ticketing systems Experience with cloud platforms, including Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform, or Amazon Web Services Experience with virtualization technologies, including VMware or Hyper-V Experience with Zero Trust security principles Experience with Windows Server and Linux operating systems
